query:
You are working on the Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime repository.
The codebase is at version 0.6 (commit: cc8121a6).

## Issue to Resolve

Potential improvements and input preservation in parsing and formatting error messages
I've set the experiment "I have a system with externally-supplied input that I'm parsing" in order to see how debuggable the end system is.

Here are a few potential improvements that can be made:

1) `Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker` correctly shows an unexpected component, but it would be nice to see the full input to understand the source of the problem.

E.g. for `Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker: hour is 23, but the AM/PM marker is AM` `23` is nice, but for observability purposes, I would like to see the whole input.


2) `Position X: Expected One of` probably should be `Position 0: Expected one of`


3) The same as for `1` it goes for validation of components done in constructors: `LocalDate`, `LocalDateTime`, `LocalTime`, `UtcOffset`

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `core/common/src/LocalDate.kt`
- `core/common/src/LocalDateTime.kt`
- `core/common/src/LocalTime.kt`
- `core/common/src/UtcOffset.kt`
- `core/common/src/format/DateTimeComponents.kt`
- `core/common/src/format/DateTimeFormat.kt`
- `core/common/src/internal/format/FieldFormatDirective.kt`
- `core/common/src/internal/format/FormatStructure.kt`
- `core/common/src/internal/format/parser/ParserOperation.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

  6â†’package kotlinx.datetime
  7â†’
  8â†’import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
  9â†’import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalDateIso8601Serializer
 10â†’import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 11â†’
 12â†’/**
 13â†’ * The date part of [LocalDateTime].
 14â†’ *
 15â†’ * This class represents dates without a reference to a particular time zone.
 16â†’ * As such, these objects may denote different spans of time in different time zones: for someone in Berlin,
 17â†’ * `2020-08-30` started and ended at different moments from those for someone in Tokyo.
 18â†’ *
 19â†’ * The arithmetic on [LocalDate] values is defined independently of the time zone (so `2020-08-30` plus one day
 20â†’ * is `2020-08-31` everywhere): see various [LocalDate.plus] and [LocalDate.minus] functions, as well
 21â†’ * as [LocalDate.periodUntil] and various other [*until][LocalDate.daysUntil] functions.
 22â†’ */
 23â†’@Serializable(with = LocalDateIso8601Serializer::class)
 24â†’public expect class LocalDate : Comparable<LocalDate> {
 25â†’    public companion object {
 26â†’        /**
 27â†’         * A shortcut for calling [DateTimeFormat.parse].
 28â†’         *
 29â†’         * Parses a string that represents a date and returns the parsed [LocalDate] value.
 30â†’         *
 31â†’         * If [format] is not specified, [Formats.ISO] is used.
 32â†’         *
 33â†’         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [LocalDate] are exceeded.
 34â†’         *
 35â†’         * @see LocalDate.toString for formatting using the default format.
 36â†’         * @see LocalDate.format for formatting using a custom format.
 37â†’         */
 38â†’        public fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> = getIsoDateFormat()): LocalDate
 39â†’
 40â†’        /**
 41â†’         * Returns a [LocalDate] that is [epochDays] number of days from the epoch day `1970-01-01`.
 42â†’         *
 43â†’         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the result exceeds the platform-specific boundaries of [LocalDate].
 44â†’         *
 45â†’         * @see LocalDate.toEpochDays
 46â†’         */
 47â†’        public fun fromEpochDays(epochDays: Int): LocalDate
 48â†’
 49â†’        /**
 50â†’         * Creates a new format for parsing and formatting [LocalDate] values.
 51â†’         *
 52â†’         * Example:
 53â†’         * ```
 54â†’         * // 2020 Jan 05
 55â†’         * LocalDate.Format {
 56â†’         *   year()
 57â†’         *   char(' ')
 58â†’         *   monthName(MonthNames.ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED)
 59â†’         *   char(' ')
 60â†’         *   dayOfMonth()
 61â†’         * }
 62â†’         * ```
 63â†’         *
 64â†’         * Only parsing and formatting of well-formed values is supported. If the input does not fit the boundaries
 65â†’         * (for example, [dayOfMonth] is 31 for February), consider using [DateTimeComponents.Format] instead.
 66â†’         *
 67â†’         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [LocalDate.Formats].
 68â†’         */
 69â†’        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 70â†’        public fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>
 71â†’
 72â†’        internal val MIN: LocalDate
 73â†’        internal val MAX: LocalDate
 74â†’    }
 75â†’
 76â†’    /**
 77â†’     * A collection of predefined formats for parsing and formatting [LocalDate] values.
 78â†’     *
 79â†’     * See [LocalDate.Formats.ISO] and [LocalDate.Formats.ISO_BASIC] for popular predefined formats.
 80â†’     * [LocalDate.parse] and [LocalDate.toString] can be used as convenient shortcuts for the
 81â†’     * [LocalDate.Formats.ISO] format.
 82â†’     *
 83â†’     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [LocalDate.Format] to create a custom
 84â†’     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [LocalDate] values.
 85â†’     */
 86â†’    public object Formats {
 87â†’        /**
 88â†’         * ISO 8601 extended format, which is the format used by [LocalDate.toString] and [LocalDate.parse].
 89â†’         *
 90â†’         * Examples of dates in ISO 8601 format:
 91â†’         * - `2020-08-30`
 92â†’         * - `+12020-08-30`
 93â†’         * - `0000-08-30`
 94â†’         * - `-0001-08-30`
 95â†’         */
 96â†’        public val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>
 97â†’
 98â†’        /**
 99â†’         * ISO 8601 basic format.
100â†’         *
101â†’         * Examples of dates in ISO 8601 basic format:
102â†’         * - `20200830`
103â†’         * - `+120200830`
104â†’         * - `00000830`
105â†’         * - `-00010830`
106â†’         */
107â†’        public val ISO_BASIC: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>
108â†’    }
109â†’
110â†’    /**
111â†’     * Constructs a [LocalDate] instance from the given date components.
112â†’     *
113â†’     * The components [monthNumber] and [dayOfMonth] are 1-based.
114â†’     *
115â†’     * The supported ranges of components:
116â†’     * - [year] the range is platform dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
117â†’     *          [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE]
118â†’     * - [monthNumber] `1..12`
119â†’     * - [dayOfMonth] `1..31`, the upper bound can be less, depending on the month
120â†’     *
121â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any parameter is out of range, or if [dayOfMonth] is invalid for the
122â†’     * given [monthNumber] and [year].
123â†’     */
124â†’    public constructor(year: Int, monthNumber: Int, dayOfMonth: Int)
125â†’
126â†’    /**
127â†’     * Constructs a [LocalDate] instance from the given date components.
128â†’     *
129â†’     * The supported ranges of components:
130â†’     * - [year] the range is platform dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
131â†’     *          [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE]
132â†’     * - [month] all values of the [Month] enum
133â†’     * - [dayOfMonth] `1..31`, the upper bound can be less, depending on the month
134â†’     *
135â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any parameter is out of range, or if [dayOfMonth] is invalid for the
136â†’     * given [month] and [year].
137â†’     */
138â†’    public constructor(year: Int, month: Month, dayOfMonth: Int)
139â†’
140â†’    /** Returns the year component of the date. */
141â†’    public val year: Int
142â†’
143â†’    /** Returns the number-of-month (1..12) component of the date. */
144â†’    public val monthNumber: Int
145â†’
146â†’    /** Returns the month ([Month]) component of the date. */
147â†’    public val month: Month
148â†’
149â†’    /** Returns the day-of-month component of the date. */
150â†’    public val dayOfMonth: Int
151â†’
152â†’    /** Returns the day-of-week component of the date. */
153â†’    public val dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek
154â†’
155â†’    /** Returns the day-of-year component of the date. */
156â†’    public val dayOfYear: Int
157â†’
158â†’    /**
159â†’     * Returns the number of days since the epoch day `1970-01-01`.
160â†’     *
161â†’     * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
162â†’     *
163â†’     * @see LocalDate.fromEpochDays
164â†’     */
165â†’    public fun toEpochDays(): Int
166â†’
167â†’    /**
168â†’     * Compares `this` date with the [other] date.
169â†’     * Returns zero if this date represent the same day as the other (i.e. equal to other),
170â†’     * a negative number if this date is earlier than the other,
171â†’     * and a positive number if this date is later than the other.
172â†’     */
173â†’    public override fun compareTo(other: LocalDate): Int
174â†’
175â†’    /**
176â†’     * Converts this date to the extended ISO-8601 string representation.
177â†’     *
178â†’     * @see Formats.ISO for the format details.
179â†’     * @see parse for the dual operation: obtaining [LocalDate] from a string.
180â†’     * @see LocalDate.format for formatting using a custom format.
181â†’     */
182â†’    public override fun toString(): String
183â†’}
184â†’
185â†’/**
186â†’ * Formats this value using the given [format].
187â†’ * Equivalent to calling [DateTimeFormat.format] on [format] with `this`.
188â†’ */
189â†’public fun LocalDate.format(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>): String = format.format(this)
190â†’
191â†’/**
192â†’ * @suppress
193â†’ */
194â†’@Deprecated("Removed to support more idiomatic code. See https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime/issues/339", ReplaceWith("LocalDate.parse(this)"), DeprecationLevel.WARNING)
195â†’public fun String.toLocalDate(): LocalDate = LocalDate.parse(this)
196â†’
197â†’/**
198â†’ * Combines this date's components with the specified time components into a [LocalDateTime] value.
199â†’ *
200â†’ * For finding an instant that corresponds to the start of a date in a particular time zone consider using
201â†’ * [LocalDate.atStartOfDayIn] function because a day does not always start at the fixed time 0:00:00.
202â†’ */
203â†’public fun LocalDate.atTime(hour: Int, minute: Int, second: Int = 0, nanosecond: Int = 0): LocalDateTime =
204â†’    LocalDateTime(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth, hour, minute, second, nanosecond)
205â†’
206â†’/**
207â†’ * Combines this date's components with the specified [LocalTime] components into a [LocalDateTime] value.
208â†’ *
209â†’ * For finding an instant that corresponds to the start of a date in a particular time zone consider using
210â†’ * [LocalDate.atStartOfDayIn] function because a day does not always start at the fixed time 0:00:00.
211â†’ */
212â†’public fun LocalDate.atTime(time: LocalTime): LocalDateTime = LocalDateTime(this, time)
213â†’
214â†’
215â†’/**
216â†’ * Returns a date that is the result of adding components of [DatePeriod] to this date. The components are
217â†’ * added in the order from the largest units to the smallest, i.e. from years to days.
218â†’ *
219â†’ * @see LocalDate.periodUntil
220â†’ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value or the results of intermediate computations are too large to fit in
221â†’ * [LocalDate].
222â†’ */
223â†’public expect operator fun LocalDate.plus(period: DatePeriod): LocalDate
224â†’
225â†’/**
226â†’ * Returns a date that is the result of subtracting components of [DatePeriod] from this date. The components are
227â†’ * subtracted in the order from the largest units to the smallest, i.e. from years to days.
228â†’ *
229â†’ * @see LocalDate.periodUntil
230â†’ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value or the results of intermediate computations are too large to fit in
231â†’ * [LocalDate].
232â†’ */
233â†’public operator fun LocalDate.minus(period: DatePeriod): LocalDate =
234â†’    if (period.days != Int.MIN_VALUE && period.months != Int.MIN_VALUE) {
235â†’        plus(with(period) { DatePeriod(-years, -months, -days) })
236â†’    } else {
237â†’        minus(period.years, DateTimeUnit.YEAR).minus(period.months, DateTimeUnit.MONTH)
238â†’            .minus(period.days, DateTimeUnit.DAY)
239â†’    }
240â†’
241â†’/**
242â†’ * Returns a [DatePeriod] representing the difference between `this` and [other] dates.
243â†’ *
244â†’ * The components of [DatePeriod] are calculated so that adding it to `this` date results in the [other] date.
245â†’ *
246â†’ * All components of the [DatePeriod] returned are:
247â†’ * - positive or zero if this date is earlier than the other,
248â†’ * - negative or zero if this date is later than the other,
249â†’ * - exactly zero if this date is equal to the other.
250â†’ *
251â†’ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the number of months between the two dates exceeds an Int (JVM only).
252â†’ *
253â†’ * @see LocalDate.minus
254â†’ */
255â†’public expect fun LocalDate.periodUntil(other: LocalDate): DatePeriod
256â†’
257â†’/**
258â†’ * Returns a [DatePeriod] representing the difference between [other] and `this` dates.
259â†’ *
260â†’ * The components of [DatePeriod] are calculated so that adding it back to the `other` date results in this date.
261â†’ *
262â†’ * All components of the [DatePeriod] returned are:
263â†’ * - negative or zero if this date is earlier than the other,
264â†’ * - positive or zero if this date is later than the other,
265â†’ * - exactly zero if this date is equal to the other.
266â†’ *
267â†’ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the number of months between the two dates exceeds an Int (JVM only).
268â†’ *
269â†’ * @see LocalDate.periodUntil
270â†’ */
271â†’public operator fun LocalDate.minus(other: LocalDate): DatePeriod = other.periodUntil(this)
272â†’
273â†’/**
274â†’ * Returns the whole number of the specified date [units][unit] between `this` and [other] dates.
275â†’ *
276â†’ * The value returned is:
277â†’ * - positive or zero if this date is earlier than the other,
278â†’ * - negative or zero if this date is later than the other,
279â†’ * - zero if this date is equal to the other.
280â†’
281â†’ * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
282â†’ *
283â†’ * @see LocalDate.daysUntil
284â†’ * @see LocalDate.monthsUntil
285â†’ * @see LocalDate.yearsUntil
286â†’ *
287â†’ */
288â†’public expect fun LocalDate.until(other: LocalDate, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): Int
289â†’
290â†’/**
291â†’ * Returns the number of whole days between two dates.
292â†’ *
293â†’ * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
294â†’ *
295â†’ * @see LocalDate.until
296â†’ */
297â†’public expect fun LocalDate.daysUntil(other: LocalDate): Int
298â†’
299â†’/**
300â†’ * Returns the number of whole months between two dates.
301â†’ *
302â†’ * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
303â†’ *
304â†’ * @see LocalDate.until
305â†’ */
306â†’public expect fun LocalDate.monthsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int
307â†’
308â†’/**
309â†’ * Returns the number of whole years between two dates.
310â†’ *
311â†’ * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
312â†’ *
313â†’ * @see LocalDate.until
314â†’ */
315â†’public expect fun LocalDate.yearsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int
316â†’
317â†’/**
318â†’ * Returns a [LocalDate] that is the result of adding one [unit] to this date.
319â†’ *
320â†’ * The returned date is later than this date.
321â†’ *
322â†’ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the result exceeds the boundaries of [LocalDate].
323â†’ */
324â†’@Deprecated("Use the plus overload with an explicit number of units", ReplaceWith("this.plus(1, unit)"))
325â†’public expect fun LocalDate.plus(unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate
326â†’
327â†’/**
328â†’ * Returns a [LocalDate] that is the result of subtracting one [unit] from this date.
329â†’ *
330â†’ * The returned date is earlier than this date.
331â†’ *
332â†’ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the result exceeds the boundaries of [LocalDate].
333â†’ */
334â†’@Deprecated("Use the minus overload with an explicit number of units", ReplaceWith("this.minus(1, unit)"))
335â†’public fun LocalDate.minus(unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate = plus(-1, unit)
336â†’
337â†’/**
338â†’ * Returns a [LocalDate] that is the result of adding the [value] number of the specified [unit] to this date.
339â†’ *
340â†’ * If the [value] is positive, the returned date is later than this date.
341â†’ * If the [value] is negative, the returned date is earlier than this date.
342â†’ *
343â†’ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the result exceeds the boundaries of [LocalDate].
344â†’ */
345â†’public expect fun LocalDate.plus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate
346â†’
347â†’/**
348â†’ * Returns a [LocalDate] that is the result of subtracting the [value] number of the specified [unit] from this date.
349â†’ *
350â†’ * If the [value] is positive, the returned date is earlier than this date.
351â†’ * If the [value] is negative, the returned date is later than this date.
352â†’ *
353â†’ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the result exceeds the boundaries of [LocalDate].
354â†’ */
355â†’public expect fun LocalDate.minus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate
356â†’
357â†’/**
358â†’ * Returns a [LocalDate] that is the result of adding the [value] number of the specified [unit] to this date.
359â†’ *
360â†’ * If the [value] is positive, the returned date is later than this date.
361â†’ * If the [value] is negative, the returned date is earlier than this date.
362â†’ *
363â†’ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the result exceeds the boundaries of [LocalDate].
364â†’ */
365â†’public expect fun LocalDate.plus(value: Long, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate
366â†’
367â†’/**
368â†’ * Returns a [LocalDate] that is the result of subtracting the [value] number of the specified [unit] from this date.
369â†’ *
370â†’ * If the [value] is positive, the returned date is earlier than this date.
371â†’ * If the [value] is negative, the returned date is later than this date.
372â†’ *
373â†’ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the result exceeds the boundaries of [LocalDate].
374â†’ */
375â†’public fun LocalDate.minus(value: Long, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate = plus(-value, unit)
376â†’
377â†’// workaround for https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-65484
378â†’internal fun getIsoDateFormat() = LocalDate.Formats.ISO
379â†’

1â†’/*
  2â†’ * Copyright 2019-2022 JetBrains s.r.o. and contributors.
  3â†’ * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4â†’ */
  5â†’
  6â†’package kotlinx.datetime
  7â†’
  8â†’import kotlinx.datetime.LocalDate.Companion.parse
  9â†’import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
 10â†’import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalDateTimeIso8601Serializer
 11â†’import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 12â†’
 13â†’/**
 14â†’ * The representation of a specific civil date and time without a reference to a particular time zone.
 15â†’ *
 16â†’ * This class does not describe specific *moments in time*, which are represented as [Instant] values.
 17â†’ * Instead, its instances can be thought of as clock readings, something that an observer in a particular time zone
 18â†’ * could witness.
 19â†’ * For example, `2020-08-30T18:43` is not a *moment in time*, since someone in Berlin and someone in Tokyo would witness
 20â†’ * this on their clocks at different times.
 21â†’ *
 22â†’ * The main purpose of this class is to provide human-readable representations of [Instant] values, or to transfer them
 23â†’ * as data.
 24â†’ *
 25â†’ * The arithmetic on [LocalDateTime] values is not provided, since without accounting for the time zone transitions it may give misleading results.
 26â†’ */
 27â†’@Serializable(with = LocalDateTimeIso8601Serializer::class)
 28â†’public expect class LocalDateTime : Comparable<LocalDateTime> {
 29â†’    public companion object {
 30â†’
 31â†’        /**
 32â†’         * A shortcut for calling [DateTimeFormat.parse].
 33â†’         *
 34â†’         * Parses a string that represents a date/time value including date and time components
 35â†’         * but without any time zone component and returns the parsed [LocalDateTime] value.
 36â†’         *
 37â†’         * If [format] is not specified, [Formats.ISO] is used.
 38â†’         *
 39â†’         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [LocalDateTime] are
 40â†’         * exceeded.
 41â†’         */
 42â†’        public fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime> = getIsoDateTimeFormat()): LocalDateTime
 43â†’
 44â†’        /**
 45â†’         * Creates a new format for parsing and formatting [LocalDateTime] values.
 46â†’         *
 47â†’         * Examples:
 48â†’         * ```
 49â†’         * // `2020-08-30 18:43:13`, using predefined date and time formats
 50â†’         * LocalDateTime.Format { date(LocalDate.Formats.ISO); char(' '); time(LocalTime.Formats.ISO) }
 51â†’         *
 52â†’         * // `08/30 18:43:13`, using a custom format:
 53â†’         * LocalDateTime.Format {
 54â†’         *   monthNumber(); char('/'); dayOfMonth()
 55â†’         *   char(' ')
 56â†’         *   hour(); char(':'); minute()
 57â†’         *   optional { char(':'); second() }
 58â†’         * }
 59â†’         * ```
 60â†’         *
 61â†’         * Only parsing and formatting of well-formed values is supported. If the input does not fit the boundaries
 62â†’         * (for example, [dayOfMonth] is 31 for February), consider using [DateTimeComponents.Format] instead.
 63â†’         *
 64â†’         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [LocalDateTime.Formats].
 65â†’         */
 66â†’        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 67â†’        public fun Format(builder: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTime.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime>
 68â†’
 69â†’        internal val MIN: LocalDateTime
 70â†’        internal val MAX: LocalDateTime
 71â†’    }
 72â†’
 73â†’    /**
 74â†’     * A collection of predefined formats for parsing and formatting [LocalDateTime] values.
 75â†’     *
 76â†’     * [LocalDateTime.Formats.ISO] is a popular predefined format.
 77â†’     *
 78â†’     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [LocalDateTime.Format] to create a custom
 79â†’     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [LocalDateTime] values.
 80â†’     */
 81â†’    public object Formats {
 82â†’        /**
 83â†’         * ISO 8601 extended format.
 84â†’         *
 85â†’         * Examples of date/time in ISO 8601 format:
 86â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:43`
 87â†’         * - `+12020-08-30T18:43:00`
 88â†’         * - `0000-08-30T18:43:00.5`
 89â†’         * - `-0001-08-30T18:43:00.123456789`
 90â†’         *
 91â†’         * When formatting, seconds are always included, even if they are zero.
 92â†’         * Fractional parts of the second are included if non-zero.
 93â†’         *
 94â†’         * Guaranteed to parse all strings that [LocalDateTime.toString] produces.
 95â†’         */
 96â†’        public val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime>
 97â†’    }
 98â†’
 99â†’    /**
100â†’     * Constructs a [LocalDateTime] instance from the given date and time components.
101â†’     *
102â†’     * The components [monthNumber] and [dayOfMonth] are 1-based.
103â†’     *
104â†’     * The supported ranges of components:
105â†’     * - [year] the range is platform dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
106â†’     *          [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE]
107â†’     * - [monthNumber] `1..12`
108â†’     * - [dayOfMonth] `1..31`, the upper bound can be less, depending on the month
109â†’     * - [hour] `0..23`
110â†’     * - [minute] `0..59`
111â†’     * - [second] `0..59`
112â†’     * - [nanosecond] `0..999_999_999`
113â†’     *
114â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any parameter is out of range, or if [dayOfMonth] is invalid for the given [monthNumber] and
115â†’     * [year].
116â†’     */
117â†’    public constructor(
118â†’        year: Int,
119â†’        monthNumber: Int,
120â†’        dayOfMonth: Int,
121â†’        hour: Int,
122â†’        minute: Int,
123â†’        second: Int = 0,
124â†’        nanosecond: Int = 0
125â†’    )
126â†’
127â†’    /**
128â†’     * Constructs a [LocalDateTime] instance from the given date and time components.
129â†’     *
130â†’     * The supported ranges of components:
131â†’     * - [year] the range is platform dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
132â†’     *          [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE]
133â†’     * - [month] all values of the [Month] enum
134â†’     * - [dayOfMonth] `1..31`, the upper bound can be less, depending on the month
135â†’     * - [hour] `0..23`
136â†’     * - [minute] `0..59`
137â†’     * - [second] `0..59`
138â†’     * - [nanosecond] `0..999_999_999`
139â†’     *
140â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any parameter is out of range, or if [dayOfMonth] is invalid for the given [month] and
141â†’     * [year].
142â†’     */
143â†’    public constructor(
144â†’        year: Int,
145â†’        month: Month,
146â†’        dayOfMonth: Int,
147â†’        hour: Int,
148â†’        minute: Int,
149â†’        second: Int = 0,
150â†’        nanosecond: Int = 0
151â†’    )
152â†’
153â†’    /**
154â†’     * Constructs a [LocalDateTime] instance by combining the given [date] and [time] parts.
155â†’     */
156â†’    public constructor(date: LocalDate, time: LocalTime)
157â†’
158â†’    /** Returns the year component of the date. */
159â†’    public val year: Int
160â†’
161â†’    /** Returns the number-of-month (1..12) component of the date. */
162â†’    public val monthNumber: Int
163â†’
164â†’    /** Returns the month ([Month]) component of the date. */
165â†’    public val month: Month
166â†’
167â†’    /** Returns the day-of-month component of the date. */
168â†’    public val dayOfMonth: Int
169â†’
170â†’    /** Returns the day-of-week component of the date. */
171â†’    public val dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek
172â†’
173â†’    /** Returns the day-of-year component of the date. */
174â†’    public val dayOfYear: Int
175â†’
176â†’    /** Returns the hour-of-day time component of this date/time value. */
177â†’    public val hour: Int
178â†’
179â†’    /** Returns the minute-of-hour time component of this date/time value. */
180â†’    public val minute: Int
181â†’
182â†’    /** Returns the second-of-minute time component of this date/time value. */
183â†’    public val second: Int
184â†’
185â†’    /** Returns the nanosecond-of-second time component of this date/time value. */
186â†’    public val nanosecond: Int
187â†’
188â†’    /** Returns the date part of this date/time value. */
189â†’    public val date: LocalDate
190â†’
191â†’    /** Returns the time part of this date/time value. */
192â†’    public val time: LocalTime
193â†’
194â†’    /**
195â†’     * Compares `this` date/time value with the [other] date/time value.
196â†’     * Returns zero if this value is equal to the other,
197â†’     * a negative number if this value represents earlier civil time than the other,
198â†’     * and a positive number if this value represents later civil time than the other.
199â†’     */
200â†’    // TODO: add a note about pitfalls of comparing localdatetimes falling in the Autumn transition
201â†’    public override operator fun compareTo(other: LocalDateTime): Int
202â†’
203â†’    /**
204â†’     * Converts this date/time value to the ISO 8601 string representation.
205â†’     *
206â†’     * For readability, if the time represents a round minute (without seconds or fractional seconds),
207â†’     * the string representation will not include seconds. Also, fractions of seconds will add trailing zeros to
208â†’     * the fractional part until its length is a multiple of three.
209â†’     *
210â†’     * Examples of output:
211â†’     * - `2020-08-30T18:43`
212â†’     * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00`
213â†’     * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00.500`
214â†’     * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00.123456789`
215â†’     *
216â†’     * @see LocalTime.toString for details of how the time part is formatted.
217â†’     * @see Formats.ISO for a very similar format. The difference is that [Formats.ISO] will always include seconds,
218â†’     * even if they are zero, and will not add trailing zeros to the fractional part of the second for readability.
219â†’     * @see parse for the dual operation: obtaining [LocalDateTime] from a string.
220â†’     * @see LocalDateTime.format for formatting using a custom format.
221â†’     */
222â†’    public override fun toString(): String
223â†’}
224â†’
225â†’/**
226â†’ * Formats this value using the given [format].
227â†’ * Equivalent to calling [DateTimeFormat.format] on [format] with `this`.
228â†’ */
229â†’public fun LocalDateTime.format(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime>): String = format.format(this)
230â†’
231â†’/**
232â†’ * @suppress
233â†’ */
234â†’@Deprecated("Removed to support more idiomatic code. See https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime/issues/339", ReplaceWith("LocalDateTime.parse(this)"), DeprecationLevel.WARNING)
235â†’public fun String.toLocalDateTime(): LocalDateTime = LocalDateTime.parse(this)
236â†’
237â†’// workaround for https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-65484
238â†’internal fun getIsoDateTimeFormat() = LocalDateTime.Formats.ISO
239â†’

1â†’/*
  2â†’ * Copyright 2019-2022 JetBrains s.r.o. and contributors.
  3â†’ * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4â†’ */
  5â†’
  6â†’package kotlinx.datetime
  7â†’
  8â†’import kotlinx.datetime.LocalDate.Companion.parse
  9â†’import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
 10â†’import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalTimeIso8601Serializer
 11â†’import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 12â†’
 13â†’
 14â†’/**
 15â†’ * The time part of [LocalDateTime].
 16â†’ *
 17â†’ * This class represents time-of-day without a referencing a specific date.
 18â†’ * To reconstruct a full [LocalDateTime], representing civil date and time, [LocalTime] needs to be
 19â†’ * combined with [LocalDate] via [LocalDate.atTime] or [LocalTime.atDate].
 20â†’ *
 21â†’ * Also, [LocalTime] does not reference a particular time zone.
 22â†’ * Therefore, even on the same date, [LocalTime] denotes different moments of time.
 23â†’ * For example, `18:43` happens at different moments in Berlin and in Tokyo.
 24â†’ *
 25â†’ * The arithmetic on [LocalTime] values is not provided, since without accounting for the time zone
 26â†’ * transitions it may give misleading results.
 27â†’ */
 28â†’@Serializable(LocalTimeIso8601Serializer::class)
 29â†’public expect class LocalTime : Comparable<LocalTime> {
 30â†’    public companion object {
 31â†’
 32â†’        /**
 33â†’         * A shortcut for calling [DateTimeFormat.parse].
 34â†’         *
 35â†’         * Parses a string that represents time-of-day and returns the parsed [LocalTime] value.
 36â†’         *
 37â†’         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [LocalTime] are
 38â†’         * exceeded.
 39â†’         *
 40â†’         * @see LocalTime.toString for formatting using the default format.
 41â†’         * @see LocalTime.format for formatting using a custom format.
 42â†’         */
 43â†’        public fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalTime> = getIsoTimeFormat()): LocalTime
 44â†’
 45â†’        /**
 46â†’         * Constructs a [LocalTime] that represents the specified number of seconds since the start of a calendar day.
 47â†’         * The fractional parts of the second will be zero.
 48â†’         *
 49â†’         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if [secondOfDay] is outside the `0 until 86400` range,
 50â†’         * with 86400 being the number of seconds in a calendar day.
 51â†’         *
 52â†’         * @see LocalTime.toSecondOfDay
 53â†’         * @see LocalTime.fromMillisecondOfDay
 54â†’         * @see LocalTime.fromNanosecondOfDay
 55â†’         */
 56â†’        public fun fromSecondOfDay(secondOfDay: Int): LocalTime
 57â†’
 58â†’        /**
 59â†’         * Constructs a [LocalTime] that represents the specified number of milliseconds since the start of
 60â†’         * a calendar day.
 61â†’         * The sub-millisecond parts of the `LocalTime` will be zero.
 62â†’         *
 63â†’         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if [millisecondOfDay] is outside the `0 until 86400 * 1_000` range,
 64â†’         * with 86400 being the number of seconds in a calendar day.
 65â†’         *
 66â†’         * @see LocalTime.fromSecondOfDay
 67â†’         * @see LocalTime.toMillisecondOfDay
 68â†’         * @see LocalTime.fromNanosecondOfDay
 69â†’         */
 70â†’        public fun fromMillisecondOfDay(millisecondOfDay: Int): LocalTime
 71â†’
 72â†’        /**
 73â†’         * Constructs a [LocalTime] that represents the specified number of nanoseconds since the start of
 74â†’         * a calendar day.
 75â†’         *
 76â†’         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if [nanosecondOfDay] is outside the `0 until 86400 * 1_000_000_000` range,
 77â†’         * with 86400 being the number of seconds in a calendar day.
 78â†’         *
 79â†’         * @see LocalTime.fromSecondOfDay
 80â†’         * @see LocalTime.fromMillisecondOfDay
 81â†’         * @see LocalTime.toNanosecondOfDay
 82â†’         */
 83â†’        public fun fromNanosecondOfDay(nanosecondOfDay: Long): LocalTime
 84â†’
 85â†’        /**
 86â†’         * Creates a new format for parsing and formatting [LocalTime] values.
 87â†’         *
 88â†’         * Example:
 89â†’         * ```
 90â†’         * LocalTime.Format {
 91â†’         *   hour(); char(':'); minute(); char(':'); second()
 92â†’         *   optional { char('.'); secondFraction() }
 93â†’         * }
 94â†’         * ```
 95â†’         *
 96â†’         * Only parsing and formatting of well-formed values is supported. If the input does not fit the boundaries
 97â†’         * (for example, [second] is 60), consider using [DateTimeComponents.Format] instead.
 98â†’         *
 99â†’         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [LocalTime.Formats].
100â†’         */
101â†’        @Suppress("FunctionName")
102â†’        public fun Format(builder: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalTime>
103â†’
104â†’        internal val MIN: LocalTime
105â†’        internal val MAX: LocalTime
106â†’    }
107â†’
108â†’    /**
109â†’     * A collection of predefined formats for parsing and formatting [LocalDateTime] values.
110â†’     *
111â†’     * [LocalTime.Formats.ISO] is a popular predefined format.
112â†’     *
113â†’     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [LocalTime.Format] to create a custom
114â†’     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [LocalTime] values.
115â†’     */
116â†’    public object Formats {
117â†’        /**
118â†’         * ISO 8601 extended format.
119â†’         *
120â†’         * Examples: `12:34`, `12:34:56`, `12:34:56.789`, `12:34:56.1234`.
121â†’         *
122â†’         * When formatting, seconds are always included, even if they are zero.
123â†’         * Fractional parts of the second are included if non-zero.
124â†’         *
125â†’         * Guaranteed to parse all strings that [LocalTime.toString] produces.
126â†’         */
127â†’        public val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalTime>
128â†’    }
129â†’
130â†’    /**
131â†’     * Constructs a [LocalTime] instance from the given time components.
132â†’     *
133â†’     * The supported ranges of components:
134â†’     * - [hour] `0..23`
135â†’     * - [minute] `0..59`
136â†’     * - [second] `0..59`
137â†’     * - [nanosecond] `0..999_999_999`
138â†’     *
139â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any parameter is out of range.
140â†’     */
141â†’    public constructor(hour: Int, minute: Int, second: Int = 0, nanosecond: Int = 0)
142â†’
143â†’    /** Returns the hour-of-day time component of this time value. */
144â†’    public val hour: Int
145â†’
146â†’    /** Returns the minute-of-hour time component of this time value. */
147â†’    public val minute: Int
148â†’
149â†’    /** Returns the second-of-minute time component of this time value. */
150â†’    public val second: Int
151â†’
152â†’    /** Returns the nanosecond-of-second time component of this time value. */
153â†’    public val nanosecond: Int
154â†’
155â†’    /** Returns the time as a second of a day, in `0 until 24 * 60 * 60`. */
156â†’    public fun toSecondOfDay(): Int
157â†’
158â†’    /** Returns the time as a millisecond of a day, in `0 until 24 * 60 * 60 * 1_000`. */
159â†’    public fun toMillisecondOfDay(): Int
160â†’
161â†’    /** Returns the time as a nanosecond of a day, in `0 until 24 * 60 * 60 * 1_000_000_000`. */
162â†’    public fun toNanosecondOfDay(): Long
163â†’
164â†’    /**
165â†’     * Compares `this` time value with the [other] time value.
166â†’     * Returns zero if this value is equal to the other, a negative number if this value occurs earlier
167â†’     * in the course of a typical day than the other, and a positive number if this value occurs
168â†’     * later in the course of a typical day than the other.
169â†’     *
170â†’     * Note that, on days when there is a time overlap (for example, due to the daylight saving time
171â†’     * transitions in autumn), a "lesser" wall-clock reading can, in fact, happen later than the
172â†’     * "greater" one.
173â†’     */
174â†’    public override operator fun compareTo(other: LocalTime): Int
175â†’
176â†’    /**
177â†’     * Converts this time value to the extended ISO-8601 string representation.
178â†’     *
179â†’     * For readability, if the time represents a round minute (without seconds or fractional seconds),
180â†’     * the string representation will not include seconds. Also, fractions of seconds will add trailing zeros to
181â†’     * the fractional part until the number of digits after the dot is a multiple of three.
182â†’     *
183â†’     * Examples of output:
184â†’     * - `18:43`
185â†’     * - `18:43:00`
186â†’     * - `18:43:00.500`
187â†’     * - `18:43:00.123456789`
188â†’     *
189â†’     * @see Formats.ISO for a very similar format. The difference is that [Formats.ISO] will always include seconds,
190â†’     * even if they are zero, and will not add trailing zeros to the fractional part of the second for readability.
191â†’     * @see parse for the dual operation: obtaining [LocalTime] from a string.
192â†’     * @see LocalTime.format for formatting using a custom format.
193â†’     */
194â†’    public override fun toString(): String
195â†’}
196â†’
197â†’/**
198â†’ * Formats this value using the given [format].
199â†’ * Equivalent to calling [DateTimeFormat.format] on [format] with `this`.
200â†’ */
201â†’public fun LocalTime.format(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalTime>): String = format.format(this)
202â†’
203â†’/**
204â†’ * @suppress
205â†’ */
206â†’@Deprecated("Removed to support more idiomatic code. See https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime/issues/339", ReplaceWith("LocalTime.parse(this)"), DeprecationLevel.WARNING)
207â†’public fun String.toLocalTime(): LocalTime = LocalTime.parse(this)
208â†’
209â†’/**
210â†’ * Combines this time's components with the specified date components into a [LocalDateTime] value.
211â†’ */
212â†’public fun LocalTime.atDate(year: Int, monthNumber: Int, dayOfMonth: Int = 0): LocalDateTime =
213â†’    LocalDateTime(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth, hour, minute, second, nanosecond)
214â†’
215â†’/**
216â†’ * Combines this time's components with the specified date components into a [LocalDateTime] value.
217â†’ */
218â†’public fun LocalTime.atDate(year: Int, month: Month, dayOfMonth: Int = 0): LocalDateTime =
219â†’    LocalDateTime(year, month, dayOfMonth, hour, minute, second, nanosecond)
220â†’
221â†’/**
222â†’ * Combines this time's components with the specified [LocalDate] components into a [LocalDateTime] value.
223â†’ */
224â†’public fun LocalTime.atDate(date: LocalDate): LocalDateTime = LocalDateTime(date, this)
225â†’
226â†’// workaround for https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-65484
227â†’internal fun getIsoTimeFormat() = LocalTime.Formats.ISO

1â†’/*

  4â†’ */
  5â†’
  6â†’package kotlinx.datetime
  7â†’
  8â†’import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
  9â†’import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.UtcOffsetSerializer
 10â†’import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 11â†’
 12â†’/**
 13â†’ * An offset from UTC.
 14â†’ *
 15â†’ * Examples of these values:
 16â†’ * - `Z`, an offset of zero;
 17â†’ * - `+05`, plus five hours;
 18â†’ * - `-02`, minus two hours;
 19â†’ * - `+03:30`, plus three hours and thirty minutes;
 20â†’ * - `+01:23:45`, plus one hour, 23 minutes, and 45 seconds.
 21â†’ */
 22â†’@Serializable(with = UtcOffsetSerializer::class)
 23â†’public expect class UtcOffset {
 24â†’    /**
 25â†’     * The number of seconds from UTC.
 26â†’     *
 27â†’     * The larger the value, the earlier some specific civil date/time happens with the offset.
 28â†’     */
 29â†’    public val totalSeconds: Int
 30â†’
 31â†’    // TODO: Declare and document toString/equals/hashCode
 32â†’
 33â†’    public companion object {
 34â†’        /**
 35â†’         * The zero offset from UTC, `Z`.
 36â†’         */
 37â†’        public val ZERO: UtcOffset
 38â†’
 39â†’        /**
 40â†’         * A shortcut for calling [DateTimeFormat.parse].
 41â†’         *
 42â†’         * Parses a string that represents a UTC offset and returns the parsed [UtcOffset] value.
 43â†’         *
 44â†’         * If [format] is not specified, [Formats.ISO] is used.
 45â†’         *
 46â†’         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [UtcOffset] are
 47â†’         * exceeded.
 48â†’         */
 49â†’        public fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset> = getIsoUtcOffestFormat()): UtcOffset
 50â†’
 51â†’        /**
 52â†’         * Creates a new format for parsing and formatting [UtcOffset] values.
 53â†’         *
 54â†’         * Example:
 55â†’         * ```
 56â†’         * // `GMT` on zero, `+4:30:15`, using a custom format:
 57â†’         * UtcOffset.Format {
 58â†’         *   optional("GMT") {
 59â†’         *     offsetHours(Padding.NONE); char(':'); offsetMinutesOfHour()
 60â†’         *     optional { char(':'); offsetSecondsOfMinute() }
 61â†’         *   }
 62â†’         * }
 63â†’         * ```
 64â†’         *
 65â†’         * Since [UtcOffset] values are rarely formatted and parsed on their own,
 66â†’         * instances of [DateTimeFormat] obtained here will likely need to be passed to
 67â†’         * [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset.offset] in a format builder for a larger data structure.
 68â†’         *
 69â†’         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [UtcOffset.Formats].
 70â†’         */
 71â†’        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 72â†’        public fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>
 73â†’    }
 74â†’
 75â†’    /**
 76â†’     * A collection of predefined formats for parsing and formatting [UtcOffset] values.
 77â†’     *
 78â†’     * See [UtcOffset.Formats.ISO], [UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC], and [UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS]
 79â†’     * for popular predefined formats.
 80â†’     *
 81â†’     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [UtcOffset.Format] to create a custom
 82â†’     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [UtcOffset] values.
 83â†’     */
 84â†’    public object Formats {
 85â†’        /**
 86â†’         * ISO 8601 extended format, which is the format used by [UtcOffset.parse] and [UtcOffset.toString].
 87â†’         *
 88â†’         * An extension of the ISO 8601 is that this format allows parsing and formatting seconds.
 89â†’         *
 90â†’         * When formatting, seconds are omitted if they are zero. If the whole offset is zero, the letter `Z` is output.
 91â†’         *
 92â†’         * Examples of UTC offsets in ISO 8601 format:
 93â†’         * - `Z` or `+00:00`, an offset of zero;
 94â†’         * - `+05:00`, five hours;
 95â†’         * - `-02:00`, minus two hours;
 96â†’         * - `-17:16`
 97â†’         * - `+10:36:30`
 98â†’         */
 99â†’        public val ISO: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>
100â†’
101â†’        /**
102â†’         * ISO 8601 basic format.
103â†’         *
104â†’         * An extension of the ISO 8601 is that this format allows parsing and formatting seconds.
105â†’         *
106â†’         * When formatting, seconds are omitted if they are zero. If the whole offset is zero, the letter `Z` is output.
107â†’         *
108â†’         * Examples of UTC offsets in ISO 8601 basic format:
109â†’         * - `Z`
110â†’         * - `+05`
111â†’         * - `-1716`
112â†’         * - `+103630`
113â†’         *
114â†’         * @see UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS
115â†’         */
116â†’        public val ISO_BASIC: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>
117â†’
118â†’        /**
119â†’         * A subset of the ISO 8601 basic format that always outputs and parses exactly a numeric sign and four digits:
120â†’         * two digits for the hour and two digits for the minute. If the offset has a non-zero number of seconds,
121â†’         * they are truncated.
122â†’         *
123â†’         * Examples of UTC offsets in this format:
124â†’         * - `+0000`
125â†’         * - `+0500`
126â†’         * - `-1716`
127â†’         * - `+1036`
128â†’         *
129â†’         * @see UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC
130â†’         */
131â†’        public val FOUR_DIGITS: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>
132â†’    }
133â†’
134â†’    /**
135â†’     * Converts this UTC offset to the extended ISO-8601 string representation.
136â†’     *
137â†’     * @see Formats.ISO for the format details.
138â†’     * @see parse for the dual operation: obtaining [UtcOffset] from a string.
139â†’     * @see UtcOffset.format for formatting using a custom format.
140â†’     */
141â†’    public override fun toString(): String
142â†’}
143â†’
144â†’/**
145â†’ * Formats this value using the given [format].
146â†’ * Equivalent to calling [DateTimeFormat.format] on [format] with `this`.
147â†’ */
148â†’public fun UtcOffset.format(format: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>): String = format.format(this)
149â†’
150â†’/**
151â†’ * Constructs a [UtcOffset] from hours, minutes, and seconds components.
152â†’ *
153â†’ * All components must have the same sign.
154â†’ *
155â†’ * The bounds are checked: it is invalid to pass something other than `Â±[0; 59]` as the number of seconds or minutes.
156â†’ * For example, `UtcOffset(hours = 3, minutes = 61)` is invalid.
157â†’ *
158â†’ * However, the non-null component of the highest order can exceed these bounds,
159â†’ * for example, `UtcOffset(minutes = 241)` is valid.
160â†’ *
161â†’ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if a component exceeds its bounds when a higher order component is specified.
162â†’ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if components have different signs.
163â†’ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the resulting `UtcOffset` value is outside of range `Â±18:00`.
164â†’ */
165â†’public expect fun UtcOffset(hours: Int? = null, minutes: Int? = null, seconds: Int? = null): UtcOffset
166â†’
167â†’@Deprecated("Use UtcOffset.ZERO instead", ReplaceWith("UtcOffset.ZERO"), DeprecationLevel.ERROR)
168â†’public fun UtcOffset(): UtcOffset = UtcOffset.ZERO
169â†’
170â†’/**
171â†’ * Returns the fixed-offset time zone with the given UTC offset.
172â†’ */
173â†’public fun UtcOffset.asTimeZone(): FixedOffsetTimeZone = FixedOffsetTimeZone(this)
174â†’
175â†’// workaround for https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-65484
176â†’internal fun getIsoUtcOffestFormat() = UtcOffset.Formats.ISO
177â†’

1â†’/*
  2â†’ * Copyright 2019-2023 JetBrains s.r.o. and contributors.
  3â†’ * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4â†’ */
  5â†’
  6â†’package kotlinx.datetime.format
  7â†’
  8â†’import kotlinx.datetime.*
  9â†’import kotlinx.datetime.DayOfWeek
 10â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
 11â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.*
 12â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.Copyable
 13â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeMultiply
 14â†’import kotlin.reflect.*
 15â†’
 16â†’/**
 17â†’ * A collection of date-time fields, used specifically for parsing and formatting.
 18â†’ *
 19â†’ * Its main purpose is to provide support for complex date-time formats that don't correspond to any of the standard
 20â†’ * entities in the library. For example, a format that includes only the month and the day of the month, but not the
 21â†’ * year, can not be represented and parsed as a [LocalDate], but it is valid for a [DateTimeComponents].
 22â†’ *
 23â†’ * Example:
 24â†’ * ```
 25â†’ * val input = "2020-03-16T23:59:59.999999999+03:00"
 26â†’ * val components = DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET.parse(input)
 27â†’ * val localDateTime = components.toLocalDateTime() // LocalDateTime(2020, 3, 16, 23, 59, 59, 999_999_999)
 28â†’ * val instant = components.toInstantUsingOffset() // Instant.parse("2020-03-16T20:59:59.999999999Z")
 29â†’ * val offset = components.toUtcOffset() // UtcOffset(hours = 3)
 30â†’ * ```
 31â†’ *
 32â†’ * Another purpose is to support parsing and formatting data with out-of-bounds values. For example, parsing
 33â†’ * `23:59:60` as a [LocalTime] is not possible, but it is possible to parse it as a [DateTimeComponents], adjust the value by
 34â†’ * setting [second] to `59`, and then convert it to a [LocalTime] via [toLocalTime].
 35â†’ *
 36â†’ * Example:
 37â†’ * ```
 38â†’ * val input = "23:59:60"
 39â†’ * val extraDay: Boolean
 40â†’ * val time = DateTimeComponents.Format {
 41â†’ *   time(LocalTime.Formats.ISO)
 42â†’ * }.parse(input).apply {
 43â†’ *   if (hour == 23 && minute == 59 && second == 60) {
 44â†’ *     hour = 0; minute = 0; second = 0; extraDay = true
 45â†’ *   } else {
 46â†’ *     extraDay = false
 47â†’ *   }
 48â†’ * }.toLocalTime()
 49â†’ * ```
 50â†’ *
 51â†’ * Because this class has limited applications, constructing it directly is not possible.
 52â†’ * For formatting, use the [format] overload that accepts a lambda with a [DateTimeComponents] receiver.
 53â†’ *
 54â†’ * Example:
 55â†’ * ```
 56â†’ * // Mon, 16 Mar 2020 23:59:59 +0300
 57â†’ * DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123.format {
 58â†’ *    setDateTimeOffset(LocalDateTime(2020, 3, 16, 23, 59, 59, 999_999_999))
 59â†’ *    setDateTimeOffset(UtcOffset(hours = 3))
 60â†’ * }
 61â†’ * ```
 62â†’ *
 63â†’ * Accessing the fields of this class is not thread-safe.
 64â†’ * Make sure to apply proper synchronization if you are using a single instance from multiple threads.
 65â†’ */
 66â†’public class DateTimeComponents internal constructor(internal val contents: DateTimeComponentsContents = DateTimeComponentsContents()) {
 67â†’    public companion object {
 68â†’        /**
 69â†’         * Creates a [DateTimeFormat] for [DateTimeComponents] values using [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents].
 70â†’         *
 71â†’         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [DateTimeComponents.Formats].
 72â†’         */
 73â†’        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 74â†’        public fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents> {
 75â†’            val builder = DateTimeComponentsFormat.Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
 76â†’            block(builder)
 77â†’            return DateTimeComponentsFormat(builder.build())
 78â†’        }
 79â†’    }
 80â†’
 81â†’    /**
 82â†’     * A collection of formats for parsing and formatting [DateTimeComponents] values.
 83â†’     *
 84â†’     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [DateTimeComponents.Format] to create a custom
 85â†’     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [DateTimeComponents] values.
 86â†’     */
 87â†’    public object Formats {
 88â†’
 89â†’        /**
 90â†’         * ISO 8601 extended format for dates and times with UTC offset.
 91â†’         *
 92â†’         * For specifying the time zone offset, the format uses the [UtcOffset.Formats.ISO] format, except that during
 93â†’         * parsing, specifying the minutes is optional.
 94â†’         *
 95â†’         * This format differs from [LocalTime.Formats.ISO] in its time part in that
 96â†’         * specifying the seconds is *not* optional.
 97â†’         *
 98â†’         * Examples of instants in the ISO 8601 format:
 99â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00Z`
100â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00.50Z`
101â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00.123456789Z`
102â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:40:00+03:00`
103â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:40:00+03:30:20`
104â†’         * * `2020-01-01T23:59:59.123456789+01`
105â†’         * * `+12020-01-31T23:59:59Z`
106â†’         *
107â†’         * This format uses the local date, local time, and UTC offset fields of [DateTimeComponents].
108â†’         *
109â†’         * See ISO-8601-1:2019, 5.4.2.1b), excluding the format without the offset.
110â†’         *
111â†’         * Guaranteed to parse all strings that [Instant.toString] produces.
112â†’         */
113â†’        public val ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents> = Format {
114â†’            date(ISO_DATE)
115â†’            alternativeParsing({
116â†’                char('t')
117â†’            }) {
118â†’                char('T')
119â†’            }
120â†’            hour()
121â†’            char(':')
122â†’            minute()
123â†’            char(':')
124â†’            second()
125â†’            optional {
126â†’                char('.')
127â†’                secondFraction(1, 9)
128â†’            }
129â†’            alternativeParsing({
130â†’                offsetHours()
131â†’            }) {
132â†’                offset(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO)
133â†’            }
134â†’        }
135â†’
136â†’        /**
137â†’         * RFC 1123 format for dates and times with UTC offset.
138â†’         *
139â†’         * Examples of valid strings:
140â†’         * * `Mon, 30 Jun 2008 11:05:30 GMT`
141â†’         * * `Mon, 30 Jun 2008 11:05:30 -0300`
142â†’         * * `30 Jun 2008 11:05:30 UT`
143â†’         *
144â†’         * North American and military time zone abbreviations are not supported.
145â†’         */
146â†’        public val RFC_1123: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents> = Format {
147â†’            alternativeParsing({
148â†’                // the day of week may be missing
149â†’            }) {
150â†’                dayOfWeek(DayOfWeekNames.ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED)
151â†’                chars(", ")
152â†’            }
153â†’            dayOfMonth(Padding.NONE)
154â†’            char(' ')
155â†’            monthName(MonthNames.ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED)
156â†’            char(' ')
157â†’            year()
158â†’            char(' ')
159â†’            hour()
160â†’            char(':')
161â†’            minute()
162â†’            optional {
163â†’                char(':')
164â†’                second()
165â†’            }
166â†’            chars(" ")
167â†’            alternativeParsing({
168â†’                chars("UT")
169â†’            }, {
170â†’                chars("Z")
171â†’            }) {
172â†’                optional("GMT") {
173â†’                    offset(UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS)
174â†’                }
175â†’            }
176â†’        }
177â†’    }
178â†’
179â†’    /**
180â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [localTime] to this [DateTimeComponents].
181â†’     * The [localTime] is written to the [hour], [hourOfAmPm], [amPm], [minute], [second] and [nanosecond] fields.
182â†’     *
183â†’     * If any of the fields are already set, they will be overwritten.
184â†’     */
185â†’    public fun setTime(localTime: LocalTime) {
186â†’        contents.time.populateFrom(localTime)
187â†’    }
188â†’
189â†’    /**
190â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [localDate] to this [DateTimeComponents].
191â†’     * The [localDate] is written to the [year], [monthNumber], [dayOfMonth], and [dayOfWeek] fields.
192â†’     *
193â†’     * If any of the fields are already set, they will be overwritten.
194â†’     */
195â†’    public fun setDate(localDate: LocalDate) {
196â†’        contents.date.populateFrom(localDate)
197â†’    }
198â†’
199â†’    /**
200â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [localDateTime] to this [DateTimeComponents].
201â†’     * The [localDateTime] is written to the
202â†’     * [year], [monthNumber], [dayOfMonth], [dayOfWeek],
203â†’     * [hour], [hourOfAmPm], [amPm], [minute], [second] and [nanosecond] fields.
204â†’     *
205â†’     * If any of the fields are already set, they will be overwritten.
206â†’     */
207â†’    public fun setDateTime(localDateTime: LocalDateTime) {
208â†’        contents.date.populateFrom(localDateTime.date)
209â†’        contents.time.populateFrom(localDateTime.time)
210â†’    }
211â†’
212â†’    /**
213â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [utcOffset] to this [DateTimeComponents].
214â†’     * The [utcOffset] is written to the [offsetHours], [offsetMinutesOfHour], [offsetSecondsOfMinute], and
215â†’     * [offsetIsNegative] fields.
216â†’     *
217â†’     * If any of the fields are already set, they will be overwritten.
218â†’     */
219â†’    public fun setOffset(utcOffset: UtcOffset) {
220â†’        contents.offset.populateFrom(utcOffset)
221â†’    }
222â†’
223â†’    /**
224â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [instant] to this [DateTimeComponents].
225â†’     *
226â†’     * This method is almost always equivalent to the following code:
227â†’     * ```
228â†’     * setDateTime(instant.toLocalDateTime(offset))
229â†’     * setOffset(utcOffset)
230â†’     * ```
231â†’     * However, this also works for instants that are too large to be represented as a [LocalDateTime].
232â†’     *
233â†’     * If any of the fields are already set, they will be overwritten.
234â†’     */
235â†’    public fun setDateTimeOffset(instant: Instant, utcOffset: UtcOffset) {
236â†’        val smallerInstant = Instant.fromEpochSeconds(
237â†’            instant.epochSeconds % SECONDS_PER_10000_YEARS, instant.nanosecondsOfSecond
238â†’        )
239â†’        setDateTime(smallerInstant.toLocalDateTime(utcOffset))
240â†’        setOffset(utcOffset)
241â†’        year = year!! + ((instant.epochSeconds / SECONDS_PER_10000_YEARS) * 10000).toInt()
242â†’    }
243â†’
244â†’    /**
245â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [localDateTime] and [utcOffset] to this [DateTimeComponents].
246â†’     *
247â†’     * A shortcut for calling [setDateTime] and [setOffset] separately.
248â†’     *
249â†’     * If [localDateTime] is obtained from an [Instant] using [LocalDateTime.toInstant], it is recommended to use
250â†’     * [setDateTimeOffset] that accepts an [Instant] directly.
251â†’     */
252â†’    public fun setDateTimeOffset(localDateTime: LocalDateTime, utcOffset: UtcOffset) {
253â†’        setDateTime(localDateTime)
254â†’        setOffset(utcOffset)
255â†’    }
256â†’
257â†’    /** The year component of the date. */
258â†’    public var year: Int? by contents.date::year
259â†’
260â†’    /**
261â†’     * The number-of-month (1..12) component of the date.
262â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..99` range.
263â†’     */
264â†’    public var monthNumber: Int? by TwoDigitNumber(contents.date::monthNumber)
265â†’
266â†’    /**
267â†’     * The month ([Month]) component of the date.
268â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during getting if [monthNumber] is outside the `1..12` range.
269â†’     */
270â†’    public var month: Month?
271â†’        get() = monthNumber?.let { Month(it) }
272â†’        set(value) {
273â†’            monthNumber = value?.number
274â†’        }
275â†’
276â†’    /**
277â†’     * The day-of-month component of the date.
278â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..99` range.
279â†’     */
280â†’    public var dayOfMonth: Int? by TwoDigitNumber(contents.date::dayOfMonth)
281â†’
282â†’    /** The day-of-week component of the date. */
283â†’    public var dayOfWeek: DayOfWeek?
284â†’        get() = contents.date.isoDayOfWeek?.let { DayOfWeek(it) }
285â†’        set(value) {
286â†’            contents.date.isoDayOfWeek = value?.isoDayNumber
287â†’        }
288â†’    // /** Returns the day-of-year component of the date. */
289â†’    // public var dayOfYear: Int
290â†’
291â†’    /**
292â†’     * The hour-of-day (0..23) time component.
293â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..99` range.
294â†’     */
295â†’    public var hour: Int? by TwoDigitNumber(contents.time::hour)
296â†’
297â†’    /**
298â†’     * The 12-hour (1..12) time component.
299â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..99` range.
300â†’     * @see amPm
301â†’     */
302â†’    public var hourOfAmPm: Int? by TwoDigitNumber(contents.time::hourOfAmPm)
303â†’
304â†’    /**
305â†’     * The AM/PM state of the time component.
306â†’     * @see hourOfAmPm
307â†’     */
308â†’    public var amPm: AmPmMarker? by contents.time::amPm
309â†’
310â†’    /**
311â†’     * The minute-of-hour component.
312â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..99` range.
313â†’     */
314â†’    public var minute: Int? by TwoDigitNumber(contents.time::minute)
315â†’
316â†’    /**
317â†’     * The second-of-minute component.
318â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..99` range.
319â†’     */
320â†’    public var second: Int? by TwoDigitNumber(contents.time::second)
321â†’
322â†’    /**
323â†’     * The nanosecond-of-second component.
324â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..999_999_999` range.
325â†’     */
326â†’    public var nanosecond: Int?
327â†’        get() = contents.time.nanosecond
328â†’        set(value) {
329â†’            require(value == null || value in 0..999_999_999) {
330â†’                "Nanosecond must be in the range [0, 999_999_999]."
331â†’            }
332â†’            contents.time.nanosecond = value
333â†’        }
334â†’
335â†’    /** True if the offset is negative. */
336â†’    public var offsetIsNegative: Boolean? by contents.offset::isNegative
337â†’
338â†’    /**
339â†’     * The total amount of full hours in the UTC offset, in the range [0; 18].
340â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..99` range.
341â†’     */
342â†’    public var offsetHours: Int? by TwoDigitNumber(contents.offset::totalHoursAbs)
343â†’
344â†’    /**
345â†’     * The amount of minutes that don't add to a whole hour in the UTC offset, in the range [0; 59].
346â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..99` range.
347â†’     */
348â†’    public var offsetMinutesOfHour: Int? by TwoDigitNumber(contents.offset::minutesOfHour)
349â†’
350â†’    /**
351â†’     * The amount of seconds that don't add to a whole minute in the UTC offset, in the range [0; 59].
352â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException during assignment if the value is outside the `0..99` range.
353â†’     */
354â†’    public var offsetSecondsOfMinute: Int? by TwoDigitNumber(contents.offset::secondsOfMinute)
355â†’
356â†’    /** The timezone identifier, for example, "Europe/Berlin". */
357â†’    public var timeZoneId: String? by contents::timeZoneId
358â†’
359â†’    /**
360â†’     * Builds a [UtcOffset] from the fields in this [DateTimeComponents].
361â†’     *
362â†’     * This method uses the following fields:
363â†’     * * [offsetIsNegative] (default value is `false`)
364â†’     * * [offsetHours] (default value is 0)
365â†’     * * [offsetMinutesOfHour] (default value is 0)
366â†’     * * [offsetSecondsOfMinute] (default value is 0)
367â†’     *
368â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any of the fields has an out-of-range value.
369â†’     */
370â†’    public fun toUtcOffset(): UtcOffset = contents.offset.toUtcOffset()
371â†’
372â†’    /**
373â†’     * Builds a [LocalDate] from the fields in this [DateTimeComponents].
374â†’     *
375â†’     * This method uses the following fields:
376â†’     * * [year]
377â†’     * * [monthNumber]
378â†’     * * [dayOfMonth]
379â†’     *
380â†’     * Also, [dayOfWeek] is checked for consistency with the other fields.
381â†’     *
382â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any of the fields is missing or invalid.
383â†’     */
384â†’    public fun toLocalDate(): LocalDate = contents.date.toLocalDate()
385â†’
386â†’    /**
387â†’     * Builds a [LocalTime] from the fields in this [DateTimeComponents].
388â†’     *
389â†’     * This method uses the following fields:
390â†’     * * [hour], [hourOfAmPm], and [amPm]
391â†’     * * [minute]
392â†’     * * [second] (default value is 0)
393â†’     * * [nanosecond] (default value is 0)
394â†’     *
395â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if hours or minutes are not present, if any of the fields are invalid, or
396â†’     * [hourOfAmPm] and [amPm] are inconsistent with [hour].
397â†’     */
398â†’    public fun toLocalTime(): LocalTime = contents.time.toLocalTime()
399â†’
400â†’    /**
401â†’     * Builds a [LocalDateTime] from the fields in this [DateTimeComponents].
402â†’     *
403â†’     * This method uses the following fields:
404â†’     * * [year]
405â†’     * * [monthNumber]
406â†’     * * [dayOfMonth]
407â†’     * * [hour], [hourOfAmPm], and [amPm]
408â†’     * * [minute]
409â†’     * * [second] (default value is 0)
410â†’     * * [nanosecond] (default value is 0)
411â†’     *
412â†’     * Also, [dayOfWeek] is checked for consistency with the other fields.
413â†’     *
414â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any of the required fields are not present,
415â†’     * any of the fields are invalid, or there's inconsistency.
416â†’     *
417â†’     * @see toLocalDate
418â†’     * @see toLocalTime
419â†’     */
420â†’    public fun toLocalDateTime(): LocalDateTime = toLocalDate().atTime(toLocalTime())
421â†’
422â†’    /**
423â†’     * Builds an [Instant] from the fields in this [DateTimeComponents].
424â†’     *
425â†’     * Uses the fields required for [toLocalDateTime] and [toUtcOffset].
426â†’     *
427â†’     * Almost always equivalent to `toLocalDateTime().toInstant(toUtcOffset())`, but also accounts for cases when
428â†’     * the year is outside the range representable by [LocalDate] but not outside the range representable by [Instant].
429â†’     *
430â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any of the required fields are not present, out-of-range, or inconsistent
431â†’     * with one another.
432â†’     */
433â†’    public fun toInstantUsingOffset(): Instant {
434â†’        val offset = toUtcOffset()
435â†’        val time = toLocalTime()
436â†’        val truncatedDate = contents.date.copy()
437â†’        /**
438â†’         * 10_000 is a number that is both
439â†’         * * guaranteed to be representable as the number of years in a [LocalDate],
440â†’         * * and is a multiple of 400, which is the number of years in a leap cycle, which means that taking the
441â†’         *   remainder of the year after dividing by 40_000 will not change the leap year status of the year.
442â†’         */
443â†’        truncatedDate.year = requireParsedField(truncatedDate.year, "year") % 10_000
444â†’        val totalSeconds = try {
445â†’            val secDelta = safeMultiply((year!! / 10_000).toLong(), SECONDS_PER_10000_YEARS)
446â†’            val epochDays = truncatedDate.toLocalDate().toEpochDays().toLong()
447â†’            safeAdd(secDelta, epochDays * SECONDS_PER_DAY + time.toSecondOfDay() - offset.totalSeconds)
448â†’        } catch (e: ArithmeticException) {
449â†’            throw DateTimeFormatException("The parsed date is outside the range representable by Instant", e)
450â†’        }
451â†’        if (totalSeconds < Instant.MIN.epochSeconds || totalSeconds > Instant.MAX.epochSeconds)
452â†’            throw DateTimeFormatException("The parsed date is outside the range representable by Instant")
453â†’        return Instant.fromEpochSeconds(totalSeconds, nanosecond ?: 0)
454â†’    }
455â†’}
456â†’
457â†’/**
458â†’ * Uses this format to format an unstructured [DateTimeComponents].
459â†’ *
460â†’ * [block] is called on an initially-empty [DateTimeComponents] before formatting.
461â†’ *
462â†’ * Example:
463â†’ * ```
464â†’ * // Mon, 16 Mar 2020 23:59:59 +0300
465â†’ * DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123.format {
466â†’ *    setDateTime(LocalDateTime(2020, 3, 16, 23, 59, 59, 999_999_999))
467â†’ *    setOffset(UtcOffset(hours = 3))
468â†’ * }
469â†’ * ```
470â†’ *
471â†’ * @throws IllegalStateException if some values needed for the format are not present or can not be formatted:
472â†’ * for example, trying to format [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.monthName] using a [DateTimeComponents.monthNumber]
473â†’ * value of 20.
474â†’ */
475â†’public fun DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents>.format(block: DateTimeComponents.() -> Unit): String =
476â†’    format(DateTimeComponents().apply { block() })
477â†’
478â†’/**
479â†’ * Parses a [DateTimeComponents] from [input] using the given format.
480â†’ * Equivalent to calling [DateTimeFormat.parse] on [format] with [input].
481â†’ *
482â†’ * [DateTimeComponents] does not perform any validation, so even invalid values may be parsed successfully if the string pattern
483â†’ * matches.
484â†’ *
485â†’ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text does not match the format.
486â†’ */
487â†’public fun DateTimeComponents.Companion.parse(
488â†’    input: CharSequence,
489â†’    format: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents>
490â†’): DateTimeComponents =
491â†’    format.parse(input)
492â†’
493â†’internal class DateTimeComponentsContents internal constructor(
494â†’    val date: IncompleteLocalDate = IncompleteLocalDate(),
495â†’    val time: IncompleteLocalTime = IncompleteLocalTime(),
496â†’    val offset: IncompleteUtcOffset = IncompleteUtcOffset(),
497â†’    var timeZoneId: String? = null,
498â†’) : DateFieldContainer by date, TimeFieldContainer by time, UtcOffsetFieldContainer by offset,
499â†’    DateTimeFieldContainer, Copyable<DateTimeComponentsContents> {
500â†’    override fun copy(): DateTimeComponentsContents =
501â†’        DateTimeComponentsContents(date.copy(), time.copy(), offset.copy(), timeZoneId)
502â†’
503â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
504â†’        other is DateTimeComponentsContents && other.date == date && other.time == time &&
505â†’            other.offset == offset && other.timeZoneId == timeZoneId
506â†’
507â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int =
508â†’        date.hashCode() xor time.hashCode() xor offset.hashCode() xor (timeZoneId?.hashCode() ?: 0)
509â†’}
510â†’
511â†’internal val timeZoneField = GenericFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateTimeComponentsContents::timeZoneId))
512â†’
513â†’internal class TimeZoneIdDirective(private val knownZones: Set<String>) :
514â†’    StringFieldFormatDirective<DateTimeComponentsContents>(timeZoneField, knownZones) {
515â†’
516â†’    override val builderRepresentation: String
517â†’        get() =
518â†’            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents::timeZoneId.name}()"
519â†’
520â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is TimeZoneIdDirective && other.knownZones == knownZones
521â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = knownZones.hashCode()
522â†’}
523â†’
524â†’internal class DateTimeComponentsFormat(override val actualFormat: CachedFormatStructure<DateTimeComponentsContents>) :
525â†’    AbstractDateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents, DateTimeComponentsContents>() {
526â†’    override fun intermediateFromValue(value: DateTimeComponents): DateTimeComponentsContents = value.contents
527â†’
528â†’    override fun valueFromIntermediate(intermediate: DateTimeComponentsContents): DateTimeComponents =
529â†’        DateTimeComponents(intermediate)
530â†’
531â†’    override val emptyIntermediate get() = emptyDateTimeComponentsContents
532â†’
533â†’    class Builder(override val actualBuilder: AppendableFormatStructure<DateTimeComponentsContents>) :
534â†’        AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<DateTimeComponentsContents, Builder>, AbstractWithDateTimeBuilder,
535â†’        AbstractWithOffsetBuilder, DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents {
536â†’        override fun addFormatStructureForDateTime(structure: FormatStructure<DateTimeFieldContainer>) {
537â†’            actualBuilder.add(structure)
538â†’        }
539â†’
540â†’        override fun addFormatStructureForOffset(structure: FormatStructure<UtcOffsetFieldContainer>) {
541â†’            actualBuilder.add(structure)
542â†’        }
543â†’
544â†’        override fun timeZoneId() =
545â†’            actualBuilder.add(BasicFormatStructure(TimeZoneIdDirective(TimeZone.availableZoneIds)))
546â†’
547â†’        @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
548â†’        override fun dateTimeComponents(format: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents>) = when (format) {
549â†’            is DateTimeComponentsFormat -> actualBuilder.add(format.actualFormat)
550â†’        }
551â†’
552â†’        override fun createEmpty(): Builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
553â†’    }
554â†’}
555â†’
556â†’private class TwoDigitNumber(private val reference: KMutableProperty0<Int?>) {
557â†’    operator fun getValue(thisRef: Any?, property: KProperty<*>) = reference.getValue(thisRef, property)
558â†’
559â†’    operator fun setValue(thisRef: Any?, property: KProperty<*>, value: Int?) {
560â†’        require(value === null || value in 0..99) { "${property.name} must be a two-digit number, got '$value'" }
561â†’        reference.setValue(thisRef, property, value)
562â†’    }
563â†’}
564â†’
565â†’private val emptyDateTimeComponentsContents = DateTimeComponentsContents()
566â†’
