 12â†’/**
 13â†’ * The date part of [LocalDateTime].
 14â†’ *
 15â†’ * This class represents dates without a reference to a particular time zone.
 16â†’ * As such, these objects may denote different spans of time in different time zones: for someone in Berlin,
 17â†’ * `2020-08-30` started and ended at different moments from those for someone in Tokyo.
 18â†’ *
 19â†’ * The arithmetic on [LocalDate] values is defined independently of the time zone (so `2020-08-30` plus one day
 20â†’ * is `2020-08-31` everywhere): see various [LocalDate.plus] and [LocalDate.minus] functions, as well
 21â†’ * as [LocalDate.periodUntil] and various other [*until][LocalDate.daysUntil] functions.
 22â†’ */
 23â†’@Serializable(with = LocalDateIso8601Serializer::class)
 24â†’public expect class LocalDate : Comparable<LocalDate> {
 25â†’    public companion object {
 26â†’        /**
 27â†’         * A shortcut for calling [DateTimeFormat.parse].
 28â†’         *
 29â†’         * Parses a string that represents a date and returns the parsed [LocalDate] value.
 30â†’         *
 31â†’         * If [format] is not specified, [Formats.ISO] is used.
 32â†’         *
 33â†’         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [LocalDate] are exceeded.
 34â†’         *
 35â†’         * @see LocalDate.toString for formatting using the default format.
 36â†’         * @see LocalDate.format for formatting using a custom format.
 37â†’         */
 38â†’        public fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> = getIsoDateFormat()): LocalDate
 39â†’
 40â†’        /**
 41â†’         * Returns a [LocalDate] that is [epochDays] number of days from the epoch day `1970-01-01`.
 42â†’         *
 43â†’         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the result exceeds the platform-specific boundaries of [LocalDate].
 44â†’         *
 45â†’         * @see LocalDate.toEpochDays
 46â†’         */
 47â†’        public fun fromEpochDays(epochDays: Int): LocalDate
 48â†’
 49â†’        /**
 50â†’         * Creates a new format for parsing and formatting [LocalDate] values.
 51â†’         *
 52â†’         * Example:
 53â†’         * ```
 54â†’         * // 2020 Jan 05
 55â†’         * LocalDate.Format {
 56â†’         *   year()
 57â†’         *   char(' ')
 58â†’         *   monthName(MonthNames.ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED)
 59â†’         *   char(' ')
 60â†’         *   dayOfMonth()
 61â†’         * }
 62â†’         * ```
 63â†’         *
 64â†’         * Only parsing and formatting of well-formed values is supported. If the input does not fit the boundaries
 65â†’         * (for example, [dayOfMonth] is 31 for February), consider using [DateTimeComponents.Format] instead.
 66â†’         *
 67â†’         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [LocalDate.Formats].
 68â†’         */
 69â†’        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 70â†’        public fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>
 71â†’
 72â†’        internal val MIN: LocalDate
 73â†’        internal val MAX: LocalDate
 74â†’    }
 75â†’
 76â†’    /**
 77â†’     * A collection of predefined formats for parsing and formatting [LocalDate] values.
 78â†’     *
 79â†’     * See [LocalDate.Formats.ISO] and [LocalDate.Formats.ISO_BASIC] for popular predefined formats.
 80â†’     * [LocalDate.parse] and [LocalDate.toString] can be used as convenient shortcuts for the
 81â†’     * [LocalDate.Formats.ISO] format.
 82â†’     *
 83â†’     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [LocalDate.Format] to create a custom
 84â†’     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [LocalDate] values.
 85â†’     */
 86â†’    public object Formats {
 87â†’        /**
 88â†’         * ISO 8601 extended format, which is the format used by [LocalDate.toString] and [LocalDate.parse].
 89â†’         *
 90â†’         * Examples of dates in ISO 8601 format:
 91â†’         * - `2020-08-30`
 92â†’         * - `+12020-08-30`
 93â†’         * - `0000-08-30`
 94â†’         * - `-0001-08-30`
 95â†’         */
 96â†’        public val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>
 97â†’
 98â†’        /**
 99â†’         * ISO 8601 basic format.
100â†’         *
101â†’         * Examples of dates in ISO 8601 basic format:
102â†’         * - `20200830`
103â†’         * - `+120200830`
104â†’         * - `00000830`
105â†’         * - `-00010830`
106â†’         */
107â†’        public val ISO_BASIC: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>
108â†’    }
109â†’
110â†’    /**
111â†’     * Constructs a [LocalDate] instance from the given date components.
112â†’     *
113â†’     * The components [monthNumber] and [dayOfMonth] are 1-based.
114â†’     *
115â†’     * The supported ranges of components:
116â†’     * - [year] the range is platform dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
117â†’     *          [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE]
118â†’     * - [monthNumber] `1..12`

225â†’/**

231â†’ * [LocalDate].

131â†’     * Constructs a [LocalTime] instance from the given time components.

139â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any parameter is out of range.

  6â†’package kotlinx.datetime.format
  7â†’
  8â†’import kotlinx.datetime.*
  9â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.*
 10â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.*
 11â†’
 12â†’/**
 13â†’ * A format for parsing and formatting date-time-related values.
 14â†’ */
 15â†’public sealed interface DateTimeFormat<T> {
 16â†’    /**
 17â†’     * Formats the given [value] into a string, using this format.
 18â†’     */
 19â†’    public fun format(value: T): String
 20â†’
 21â†’    /**
 22â†’     * Formats the given [value] into the given [appendable] using this format.
 23â†’     */
 24â†’    public fun <A : Appendable> formatTo(appendable: A, value: T): A
 25â†’
 26â†’    /**
 27â†’     * Parses the given [input] string as [T] using this format.
 28â†’     *
 29â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the input string is not in the expected format or the value is invalid.
 30â†’     */
 31â†’    public fun parse(input: CharSequence): T
 32â†’
 33â†’    /**
 34â†’     * Parses the given [input] string as [T] using this format.
 35â†’     *
 36â†’     * @return the parsed value, or `null` if the input string is not in the expected format or the value is invalid.
 37â†’     */
 38â†’    public fun parseOrNull(input: CharSequence): T?
 39â†’
 40â†’    public companion object {
 41â†’        /**
 42â†’         * Produces Kotlin code that, when pasted into a Kotlin source file, creates a [DateTimeFormat] instance that
 43â†’         * behaves identically to [format].
 44â†’         *
 45â†’         * The typical use case for this is to create a [DateTimeFormat] instance using a non-idiomatic approach and
 46â†’         * then convert it to a builder DSL.
 47â†’         */
 48â†’        public fun formatAsKotlinBuilderDsl(format: DateTimeFormat<*>): String = when (format) {
 49â†’            is AbstractDateTimeFormat<*, *> -> format.actualFormat.builderString(allFormatConstants)
 50â†’        }
 51â†’    }
 52â†’}
 53â†’
 54â†’/**
 55â†’ * The style of padding to use when formatting a value.
 56â†’ */
 57â†’public enum class Padding {
 58â†’    /**
 59â†’     * No padding.
 60â†’     */
 61â†’    NONE,
 62â†’
 63â†’    /**
 64â†’     * Pad with zeros.
 65â†’     */
 66â†’    ZERO,
 67â†’
 68â†’    /**
 69â†’     * Pad with spaces.
 70â†’     */
 71â†’    SPACE
 72â†’}
 73â†’
 74â†’internal fun Padding.toKotlinCode(): String = when (this) {
 75â†’    Padding.NONE -> "Padding.NONE"
 76â†’    Padding.ZERO -> "Padding.ZERO"
 77â†’    Padding.SPACE -> "Padding.SPACE"
 78â†’}
 79â†’
 80â†’internal inline fun Padding.minDigits(width: Int) = if (this == Padding.ZERO) width else 1
 81â†’internal inline fun Padding.spaces(width: Int) = if (this == Padding.SPACE) width else null
 82â†’
 83â†’/** [T] is the user-visible type, whereas [U] is its mutable representation for parsing and formatting. */
 84â†’internal sealed class AbstractDateTimeFormat<T, U : Copyable<U>> : DateTimeFormat<T> {
 85â†’
 86â†’    abstract val actualFormat: CachedFormatStructure<U>
 87â†’
 88â†’    abstract fun intermediateFromValue(value: T): U
 89â†’
 90â†’    abstract fun valueFromIntermediate(intermediate: U): T
 91â†’
 92â†’    abstract val emptyIntermediate: U // should be part of the `Copyable` interface once the language allows this
 93â†’
 94â†’    open fun valueFromIntermediateOrNull(intermediate: U): T? = try {
 95â†’        valueFromIntermediate(intermediate)
 96â†’    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 97â†’        null
 98â†’    }
 99â†’
100â†’    override fun format(value: T): String = StringBuilder().also {
101â†’        actualFormat.formatter().format(intermediateFromValue(value), it)
102â†’    }.toString()
103â†’
104â†’    override fun <A : Appendable> formatTo(appendable: A, value: T): A = appendable.apply {
105â†’        actualFormat.formatter().format(intermediateFromValue(value), this)
106â†’    }
107â†’
108â†’    override fun parse(input: CharSequence): T {
109â†’        val matched = try {
110â†’            // without the fully qualified name, the compilation fails for some reason
111â†’            Parser(actualFormat.parser()).match(input, emptyIntermediate)
112â†’        } catch (e: ParseException) {
113â†’            throw DateTimeFormatException("Failed to parse value from '$input'", e)
114â†’        }
115â†’        try {
116â†’            return valueFromIntermediate(matched)
117â†’        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
118â†’            throw DateTimeFormatException(e.message!!)
119â†’        }
120â†’    }
121â†’
122â†’    override fun parseOrNull(input: CharSequence): T? =
123â†’        // without the fully qualified name, the compilation fails for some reason
124â†’        Parser(actualFormat.parser()).matchOrNull(input, emptyIntermediate)?.let { valueFromIntermediateOrNull(it) }
125â†’
126â†’}
127â†’
128â†’private val allFormatConstants: List<Pair<String, CachedFormatStructure<*>>> by lazy {
129â†’    fun unwrap(format: DateTimeFormat<*>): CachedFormatStructure<*> = (format as AbstractDateTimeFormat<*, *>).actualFormat
130â†’    // the formats are ordered vaguely by decreasing length, as the topmost among suitable ones is chosen.
131â†’    listOf(
132â†’        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents::dateTimeComponents.name}(DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123)" to
133â†’            unwrap(DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123),
134â†’        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents::dateTimeComponents.name}(DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET)" to
135â†’            unwrap(DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET),
136â†’        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTime::date.name}(LocalDateTime.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(LocalDateTime.Formats.ISO),
137â†’        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::date.name}(LocalDate.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(LocalDate.Formats.ISO),
138â†’        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::date.name}(LocalDate.Formats.ISO_BASIC)" to unwrap(LocalDate.Formats.ISO_BASIC),
139â†’        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::time.name}(LocalTime.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(LocalTime.Formats.ISO),
140â†’        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset::offset.name}(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO),
141â†’        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset::offset.name}(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC)" to unwrap(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC),
142â†’        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset::offset.name}(UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS)" to unwrap(UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS),
143â†’    )
144â†’}
145â†’

  9â†’import kotlinx.datetime.DayOfWeek
 10â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.*
 11â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.*
 12â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.Copyable
 13â†’import kotlinx.datetime.internal.safeMultiply
 14â†’import kotlin.reflect.*
 15â†’
 16â†’/**
 17â†’ * A collection of date-time fields, used specifically for parsing and formatting.
 18â†’ *
 19â†’ * Its main purpose is to provide support for complex date-time formats that don't correspond to any of the standard
 20â†’ * entities in the library. For example, a format that includes only the month and the day of the month, but not the
 21â†’ * year, can not be represented and parsed as a [LocalDate], but it is valid for a [DateTimeComponents].
 22â†’ *
 23â†’ * Example:
 24â†’ * ```
 25â†’ * val input = "2020-03-16T23:59:59.999999999+03:00"
 26â†’ * val components = DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET.parse(input)
 27â†’ * val localDateTime = components.toLocalDateTime() // LocalDateTime(2020, 3, 16, 23, 59, 59, 999_999_999)
 28â†’ * val instant = components.toInstantUsingOffset() // Instant.parse("2020-03-16T20:59:59.999999999Z")
 29â†’ * val offset = components.toUtcOffset() // UtcOffset(hours = 3)
 30â†’ * ```
 31â†’ *
 32â†’ * Another purpose is to support parsing and formatting data with out-of-bounds values. For example, parsing
 33â†’ * `23:59:60` as a [LocalTime] is not possible, but it is possible to parse it as a [DateTimeComponents], adjust the value by
 34â†’ * setting [second] to `59`, and then convert it to a [LocalTime] via [toLocalTime].
 35â†’ *
 36â†’ * Example:
 37â†’ * ```
 38â†’ * val input = "23:59:60"
 39â†’ * val extraDay: Boolean
 40â†’ * val time = DateTimeComponents.Format {
 41â†’ *   time(LocalTime.Formats.ISO)
 42â†’ * }.parse(input).apply {
 43â†’ *   if (hour == 23 && minute == 59 && second == 60) {
 44â†’ *     hour = 0; minute = 0; second = 0; extraDay = true
 45â†’ *   } else {
 46â†’ *     extraDay = false
 47â†’ *   }
 48â†’ * }.toLocalTime()
 49â†’ * ```
 50â†’ *
 51â†’ * Because this class has limited applications, constructing it directly is not possible.
 52â†’ * For formatting, use the [format] overload that accepts a lambda with a [DateTimeComponents] receiver.
 53â†’ *
 54â†’ * Example:
 55â†’ * ```
 56â†’ * // Mon, 16 Mar 2020 23:59:59 +0300
 57â†’ * DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123.format {
 58â†’ *    setDateTimeOffset(LocalDateTime(2020, 3, 16, 23, 59, 59, 999_999_999))
 59â†’ *    setDateTimeOffset(UtcOffset(hours = 3))
 60â†’ * }
 61â†’ * ```
 62â†’ *
 63â†’ * Accessing the fields of this class is not thread-safe.
 64â†’ * Make sure to apply proper synchronization if you are using a single instance from multiple threads.
 65â†’ */
 66â†’public class DateTimeComponents internal constructor(internal val contents: DateTimeComponentsContents = DateTimeComponentsContents()) {
 67â†’    public companion object {
 68â†’        /**
 69â†’         * Creates a [DateTimeFormat] for [DateTimeComponents] values using [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents].
 70â†’         *
 71â†’         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [DateTimeComponents.Formats].
 72â†’         */
 73â†’        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 74â†’        public fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents> {
 75â†’            val builder = DateTimeComponentsFormat.Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
 76â†’            block(builder)
 77â†’            return DateTimeComponentsFormat(builder.build())
 78â†’        }
 79â†’    }
 80â†’
 81â†’    /**
 82â†’     * A collection of formats for parsing and formatting [DateTimeComponents] values.
 83â†’     *
 84â†’     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [DateTimeComponents.Format] to create a custom
 85â†’     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [DateTimeComponents] values.
 86â†’     */
 87â†’    public object Formats {
 88â†’
 89â†’        /**
 90â†’         * ISO 8601 extended format for dates and times with UTC offset.
 91â†’         *
 92â†’         * For specifying the time zone offset, the format uses the [UtcOffset.Formats.ISO] format, except that during
 93â†’         * parsing, specifying the minutes is optional.
 94â†’         *
 95â†’         * This format differs from [LocalTime.Formats.ISO] in its time part in that
 96â†’         * specifying the seconds is *not* optional.
 97â†’         *
 98â†’         * Examples of instants in the ISO 8601 format:
 99â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00Z`
100â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00.50Z`
101â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00.123456789Z`
102â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:40:00+03:00`
103â†’         * - `2020-08-30T18:40:00+03:30:20`
104â†’         * * `2020-01-01T23:59:59.123456789+01`
105â†’         * * `+12020-01-31T23:59:59Z`
106â†’         *
107â†’         * This format uses the local date, local time, and UTC offset fields of [DateTimeComponents].
108â†’         *
109â†’         * See ISO-8601-1:2019, 5.4.2.1b), excluding the format without the offset.
110â†’         *
111â†’         * Guaranteed to parse all strings that [Instant.toString] produces.
112â†’         */
113â†’        public val ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents> = Format {
114â†’            date(ISO_DATE)
115â†’            alternativeParsing({
116â†’                char('t')
117â†’            }) {
118â†’                char('T')
119â†’            }
120â†’            hour()
121â†’            char(':')
122â†’            minute()
123â†’            char(':')
124â†’            second()
125â†’            optional {
126â†’                char('.')
127â†’                secondFraction(1, 9)
128â†’            }
129â†’            alternativeParsing({
130â†’                offsetHours()
131â†’            }) {
132â†’                offset(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO)
133â†’            }
134â†’        }
135â†’
136â†’        /**
137â†’         * RFC 1123 format for dates and times with UTC offset.
138â†’         *
139â†’         * Examples of valid strings:
140â†’         * * `Mon, 30 Jun 2008 11:05:30 GMT`
141â†’         * * `Mon, 30 Jun 2008 11:05:30 -0300`
142â†’         * * `30 Jun 2008 11:05:30 UT`
143â†’         *
144â†’         * North American and military time zone abbreviations are not supported.
145â†’         */
146â†’        public val RFC_1123: DateTimeFormat<DateTimeComponents> = Format {
147â†’            alternativeParsing({
148â†’                // the day of week may be missing
149â†’            }) {
150â†’                dayOfWeek(DayOfWeekNames.ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED)
151â†’                chars(", ")
152â†’            }
153â†’            dayOfMonth(Padding.NONE)
154â†’            char(' ')
155â†’            monthName(MonthNames.ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED)
156â†’            char(' ')
157â†’            year()
158â†’            char(' ')
159â†’            hour()
160â†’            char(':')
161â†’            minute()
162â†’            optional {
163â†’                char(':')
164â†’                second()
165â†’            }
166â†’            chars(" ")
167â†’            alternativeParsing({
168â†’                chars("UT")
169â†’            }, {
170â†’                chars("Z")
171â†’            }) {
172â†’                optional("GMT") {
173â†’                    offset(UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS)
174â†’                }
175â†’            }
176â†’        }
177â†’    }
178â†’
179â†’    /**
180â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [localTime] to this [DateTimeComponents].
181â†’     * The [localTime] is written to the [hour], [hourOfAmPm], [amPm], [minute], [second] and [nanosecond] fields.
182â†’     *
183â†’     * If any of the fields are already set, they will be overwritten.
184â†’     */
185â†’    public fun setTime(localTime: LocalTime) {
186â†’        contents.time.populateFrom(localTime)
187â†’    }
188â†’
189â†’    /**
190â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [localDate] to this [DateTimeComponents].
191â†’     * The [localDate] is written to the [year], [monthNumber], [dayOfMonth], and [dayOfWeek] fields.
192â†’     *
193â†’     * If any of the fields are already set, they will be overwritten.
194â†’     */
195â†’    public fun setDate(localDate: LocalDate) {
196â†’        contents.date.populateFrom(localDate)
197â†’    }
198â†’
199â†’    /**
200â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [localDateTime] to this [DateTimeComponents].
201â†’     * The [localDateTime] is written to the
202â†’     * [year], [monthNumber], [dayOfMonth], [dayOfWeek],
203â†’     * [hour], [hourOfAmPm], [amPm], [minute], [second] and [nanosecond] fields.
204â†’     *
205â†’     * If any of the fields are already set, they will be overwritten.
206â†’     */
207â†’    public fun setDateTime(localDateTime: LocalDateTime) {
208â†’        contents.date.populateFrom(localDateTime.date)
209â†’        contents.time.populateFrom(localDateTime.time)
210â†’    }
211â†’
212â†’    /**
213â†’     * Writes the contents of the specified [utcOffset] to this [DateTimeComponents].

 37â†’/**
 38â†’ * A directive for a decimal format of an integer field that is known to be unsigned.

 40â†’ * and the parser expects the field to be at least [minDigits] digits long.

 42â†’internal abstract class UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
 43â†’    final override val field: UnsignedFieldSpec<Target>,
 44â†’    private val minDigits: Int,
 45â†’    private val spacePadding: Int?,

 47â†’
 48â†’    private val maxDigits: Int = field.maxDigits
 49â†’
 50â†’    init {
 51â†’        require(minDigits >= 0) {
 52â†’            "The minimum number of digits ($minDigits) is negative"
 53â†’        }
 54â†’        require(maxDigits >= minDigits) {
 55â†’            "The maximum number of digits ($maxDigits) is less than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
 56â†’        }
 57â†’        if (spacePadding != null) {
 58â†’            require(spacePadding > minDigits) {
 59â†’                "The space padding ($spacePadding) should be more than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
 60â†’            }
 61â†’        }
 62â†’    }
 63â†’
 64â†’    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> {
 65â†’        val formatter = UnsignedIntFormatterStructure(
 66â†’            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
 67â†’            zeroPadding = minDigits,
 68â†’        )
 69â†’        return if (spacePadding != null) SpacePaddedFormatter(formatter, spacePadding) else formatter
 70â†’    }
 71â†’
 72â†’    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
 73â†’        spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, maxDigits, spacePadding, field.accessor, field.name)
 74â†’}

 79â†’internal abstract class NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(

 92â†’        values.getOrNull(it-field.minValue)

 96â†’    private inner class AssignableString: AssignableField<Target, String> {
 97â†’        override fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Target, newValue: String): String? =
 98â†’            field.accessor.trySetWithoutReassigning(container, values.indexOf(newValue) + field.minValue)?.let {

  6â†’package kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser
  7â†’
  8â†’internal interface ParserOperation<in Output> {
  9â†’    fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult
 10â†’}
 11â†’
 12â†’/**
 13â†’ * Consumes exactly the string [string].
 14â†’ */
 15â†’internal class PlainStringParserOperation<Output>(val string: String) : ParserOperation<Output> {
 16â†’    init {
 17â†’        require(string.isNotEmpty()) { "Empty string is not allowed" }
 18â†’        require(!string[0].isDigit()) { "String '$string' starts with a digit" }
 19â†’        require(!string[string.length - 1].isDigit()) { "String '$string' ends with a digit" }
 20â†’    }
 21â†’
 22â†’    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
 23â†’        if (startIndex + string.length > input.length)
 24â†’            return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Unexpected end of input: yet to parse '$string'" }
 25â†’        for (i in string.indices) {
 26â†’            if (input[startIndex + i] != string[i]) return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) {
 27â†’                "Expected $string but got ${input.substring(startIndex, startIndex + i + 1)}"
 28â†’            }
 29â†’        }
 30â†’        return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex + string.length)
 31â†’    }
 32â†’
 33â†’    // TODO: properly escape
 34â†’    override fun toString(): String = "'$string'"
 35â†’}
 36â†’
 37â†’/**
 38â†’ * Greedily consumes as many digits as possible and redistributes them among [consumers].
 39â†’ *
 40â†’ * At most one consumer is allowed to have a variable length.
 41â†’ * If more digits are supplied than the sum of lengths of all consumers, this is the consumer that will receive the
 42â†’ * extra.
 43â†’ */
 44â†’internal class NumberSpanParserOperation<Output>(
 45â†’    val consumers: List<NumberConsumer<Output>>,
 46â†’) : ParserOperation<Output> {
 47â†’
 48â†’    private val minLength = consumers.sumOf { it.length ?: 1 }
 49â†’    private val isFlexible = consumers.any { it.length == null }
 50â†’
 51â†’    init {
 52â†’        require(consumers.all { (it.length ?: Int.MAX_VALUE) > 0 })
 53â†’        require(consumers.count { it.length == null } <= 1)
 54â†’    }
 55â†’
 56â†’    private val whatThisExpects: String
 57â†’        get() {
 58â†’            val consumerLengths = consumers.map {
 59â†’                when (val length = it.length) {
 60â†’                    null -> "at least one digit"
 61â†’                    else -> "$length digits"
 62â†’                } + " for ${it.whatThisExpects}"
 63â†’            }
 64â†’            return if (isFlexible) {
 65â†’                "a number with at least $minLength digits: $consumerLengths"
 66â†’            } else {
 67â†’                "a number with exactly $minLength digits: $consumerLengths"
 68â†’            }
 69â†’        }
 70â†’
 71â†’    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
 72â†’        if (startIndex + minLength > input.length)
 73â†’            return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Unexpected end of input: yet to parse $whatThisExpects" }
 74â†’        var digitsInRow = 0
 75â†’        while (startIndex + digitsInRow < input.length && input[startIndex + digitsInRow].isDigit()) {
 76â†’            ++digitsInRow
 77â†’        }
 78â†’        if (digitsInRow < minLength)
 79â†’            return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) {
 80â†’                "Only found $digitsInRow digits in a row, but need to parse $whatThisExpects"
 81â†’            }
 82â†’        var index = startIndex
 83â†’        for (i in consumers.indices) {
 84â†’            val length = consumers[i].length ?: (digitsInRow - minLength + 1)
 85â†’            val numberString = input.substring(index, index + length)
 86â†’            val error = consumers[i].consume(storage, numberString)
 87â†’            if (error != null) {
 88â†’                return ParseResult.Error(index) {
 89â†’                    "Can not interpret the string '$numberString' as ${consumers[i].whatThisExpects}: ${error.errorMessage()}"
 90â†’                }
 91â†’            }
 92â†’            index += length
 93â†’        }
 94â†’        return ParseResult.Ok(index)
 95â†’    }
 96â†’
 97â†’    override fun toString(): String = whatThisExpects
 98â†’}

100â†’internal class SignParser<Output>(
101â†’    private val isNegativeSetter: (Output, Boolean) -> Unit,
102â†’    private val withPlusSign: Boolean,
103â†’    private val whatThisExpects: String,
104â†’) : ParserOperation<Output> {
105â†’    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
106â†’        if (startIndex >= input.length)
107â†’            return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex)
108â†’        val char = input[startIndex]
109â†’        if (char == '-') {
110â†’            isNegativeSetter(storage, true)
111â†’            return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex + 1)
112â†’        }
113â†’        if (char == '+' && withPlusSign) {
114â†’            isNegativeSetter(storage, false)
115â†’            return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex + 1)
116â†’        }
117â†’        return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Expected $whatThisExpects but got $char" }
118â†’    }
119â†’
120â†’    override fun toString(): String = whatThisExpects
121â†’}

128â†’) : ParserOperation<Output> {

130â†’        operation(storage)

135â†’/**
136â†’ * Matches the longest suitable string from `strings` and calls [consume] with the matched string.
137â†’ */
138â†’internal class StringSetParserOperation<Output>(
139â†’    strings: Collection<String>,
140â†’    private val setter: AssignableField<Output, String>,
141â†’    private val whatThisExpects: String,
142â†’) : ParserOperation<Output> {
143â†’
144â†’    // TODO: tries don't have good performance characteristics for small sets, add a special case for small sets
145â†’
146â†’    private class TrieNode(
147â†’        val children: MutableList<Pair<String, TrieNode>> = mutableListOf(),
148â†’        var isTerminal: Boolean = false
149â†’    )
150â†’
151â†’    private val trie = TrieNode()
152â†’
153â†’    init {
154â†’        for (string in strings) {
155â†’            var node = trie
156â†’            for (char in string) {
157â†’                val searchResult = node.children.binarySearchBy(char.toString()) { it.first }
158â†’                node = if (searchResult < 0) {
159â†’                    TrieNode().also { node.children.add(-searchResult - 1, char.toString() to it) }
160â†’                } else {
161â†’                    node.children[searchResult].second
162â†’                }
163â†’            }
164â†’            node.isTerminal = true
165â†’        }
166â†’        fun reduceTrie(trie: TrieNode) {
167â†’            for ((_, child) in trie.children) {
168â†’                reduceTrie(child)
169â†’            }
170â†’            val newChildren = mutableListOf<Pair<String, TrieNode>>()
171â†’            for ((key, child) in trie.children) {
172â†’                if (!child.isTerminal && child.children.size == 1) {
173â†’                    val (grandChildKey, grandChild) = child.children.single()
174â†’                    newChildren.add(key + grandChildKey to grandChild)
175â†’                } else {
176â†’                    newChildren.add(key to child)
177â†’                }
178â†’            }
179â†’            trie.children.clear()
180â†’            trie.children.addAll(newChildren.sortedBy { it.first })
181â†’        }
182â†’        reduceTrie(trie)
183â†’    }
184â†’
185â†’    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
186â†’        var node = trie
187â†’        var index = startIndex
188â†’        var lastMatch: Int? = null
189â†’        loop@ while (index <= input.length) {
190â†’            if (node.isTerminal) lastMatch = index
191â†’            for ((key, child) in node.children) {
192â†’                if (input.startsWith(key, index)) {
193â†’                    node = child
194â†’                    index += key.length
195â†’                    continue@loop
196â†’                }
197â†’            }
198â†’            break // nothing found
199â†’        }
200â†’        return if (lastMatch != null) {
201â†’            setter.setWithoutReassigning(storage, input.substring(startIndex, lastMatch), startIndex, lastMatch)
202â†’        } else {
203â†’            ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Expected $whatThisExpects but got ${input.substring(startIndex, index)}" }
204â†’        }
205â†’    }
206â†’}

208â†’internal fun <Output> SignedIntParser(
209â†’    minDigits: Int?,
210â†’    maxDigits: Int?,
211â†’    spacePadding: Int?,
212â†’    setter: AssignableField<Output, Int>,
213â†’    name: String,
214â†’    plusOnExceedsWidth: Int?,
215â†’): ParserStructure<Output> {
216â†’    val parsers = mutableListOf(
217â†’        spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, maxDigits, spacePadding, setter, name, withMinus = true)
218â†’    )
219â†’    if (plusOnExceedsWidth != null) {
220â†’        parsers.add(
221â†’            spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, plusOnExceedsWidth, spacePadding, setter, name)
222â†’        )
223â†’        parsers.add(
224â†’            ParserStructure(
225â†’                listOf(
226â†’                    PlainStringParserOperation("+"),
227â†’                    NumberSpanParserOperation(
228â†’                        listOf(
229â†’                            UnsignedIntConsumer(
230â†’                                plusOnExceedsWidth + 1,
231â†’                                maxDigits,
232â†’                                setter,
233â†’                                name,
234â†’                                multiplyByMinus1 = false
235â†’                            )
236â†’                        )
237â†’                    )
238â†’                ),
239â†’                emptyList()
240â†’            )
241â†’        )
242â†’    } else {
243â†’        parsers.add(
244â†’            spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, maxDigits, spacePadding, setter, name)
245â†’        )
246â†’    }
247â†’    return ParserStructure(
248â†’        emptyList(),
249â†’        parsers,
250â†’    )
251â†’}
252â†’
253â†’// With maxWidth = 4,
254â†’// padWidth = 7: "   " + (four digits | " " (three digits | " " (two digits | " ", one digit)))
255â†’// padWidth = 3: three to four digits | " " (two digits | " ", one digit)
256â†’internal fun <Target> spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(
257â†’    minDigits: Int?,
258â†’    maxDigits: Int?,
259â†’    spacePadding: Int?,
260â†’    setter: AssignableField<Target, Int>,
261â†’    name: String,
262â†’    withMinus: Boolean = false,
263â†’): ParserStructure<Target> {
264â†’    val minNumberLength = (minDigits ?: 1) + if (withMinus) 1 else 0
265â†’    val maxNumberLength = maxDigits?.let { if (withMinus) it + 1 else it } ?: Int.MAX_VALUE
266â†’    val spacePadding = spacePadding ?: 0
267â†’    fun numberOfRequiredLengths(minNumberLength: Int, maxNumberLength: Int): ParserStructure<Target> {
268â†’        check(maxNumberLength >= 1 + if (withMinus) 1 else 0)
269â†’        return ParserStructure(
270â†’            buildList {
271â†’                if (withMinus) add(PlainStringParserOperation("-"))
272â†’                add(
273â†’                    NumberSpanParserOperation(
274â†’                        listOf(
275â†’                            UnsignedIntConsumer(
276â†’                                minNumberLength - if (withMinus) 1 else 0,
277â†’                                maxNumberLength - if (withMinus) 1 else 0,
278â†’                                setter,
279â†’                                name,
280â†’                                multiplyByMinus1 = withMinus,
281â†’                            )
282â†’                        )
283â†’                    )
284â†’                )
285â†’            },
286â†’            emptyList()
287â†’        )
288â†’    }
289â†’
290â†’    val maxPaddedNumberLength = minOf(maxNumberLength, spacePadding)
291â†’    if (minNumberLength >= maxPaddedNumberLength) return numberOfRequiredLengths(minNumberLength, maxNumberLength)
292â†’    // invariant: the length of the string parsed by 'accumulated' is exactly 'accumulatedWidth'
293â†’    var accumulated: ParserStructure<Target> = numberOfRequiredLengths(minNumberLength, minNumberLength)
294â†’    for (accumulatedWidth in minNumberLength until maxPaddedNumberLength) {
295â†’        accumulated = ParserStructure(
296â†’            emptyList(),
297â†’            listOf(
298â†’                numberOfRequiredLengths(accumulatedWidth + 1, accumulatedWidth + 1),
299â†’                listOf(
300â†’                    ParserStructure(listOf(PlainStringParserOperation(" ")), emptyList()),
301â†’                    accumulated
302â†’                ).concat()
303â†’            )
304â†’        )
305â†’    }
306â†’    // accumulatedWidth == maxNumberLength || accumulatedWidth == spacePadding.
307â†’    // In the first case, we're done, in the second case, we need to add the remaining numeric lengths.
308â†’    return if (spacePadding > maxNumberLength) {
309â†’        val prepadding = PlainStringParserOperation<Target>(" ".repeat(spacePadding - maxNumberLength))
310â†’        listOf(
311â†’            ParserStructure(
312â†’                listOf(
313â†’                    prepadding,
314â†’                ),
315â†’                emptyList()
316â†’            ), accumulated
317â†’        ).concat()
318â†’    } else if (spacePadding == maxNumberLength) {
319â†’        accumulated
320â†’    } else {
321â†’        val r = ParserStructure(
322â†’            emptyList(),
323â†’            listOf(
324â†’                numberOfRequiredLengths(spacePadding + 1, maxNumberLength),
325â†’                accumulated
326â†’            )
327â†’        )
328â†’        r
329â†’    }
330â†’}

392â†’internal interface AssignableField<in Object, Type> {
393â†’    /**
394â†’     * If the field is not set, sets it to the given value.
395â†’     * If the field is set to the given value, does nothing.
396â†’     * If the field is set to a different value, throws [IllegalArgumentException].
397â†’     *
398â†’     * This function is used to ensure internal consistency during parsing.
399â†’     * There exist formats where the same data is repeated several times in the same object, for example,
400â†’     * "14:15 (02:15 PM)". In such cases, we want to ensure that the values are consistent.
401â†’     */

403â†’
404â†’    /**
405â†’     * The name of the field.
406â†’     */
407â†’    val name: String
408â†’}

410â†’private fun <Object, Type> AssignableField<Object, Type>.setWithoutReassigning(
411â†’    receiver: Object,
412â†’    value: Type,
413â†’    position: Int,
414â†’    nextIndex: Int
415â†’): ParseResult {
416â†’    val conflictingValue = trySetWithoutReassigning(receiver, value)
417â†’    return if (conflictingValue === null) {
418â†’        ParseResult.Ok(nextIndex)
419â†’    } else {
420â†’        ParseResult.Error(position) {
421â†’            "Attempting to assign conflicting values '$conflictingValue' and '$value' to field '$name'"
422â†’        }
423â†’    }
424â†’}

215â†’internal class FractionalSecondDirective(
216â†’    private val minDigits: Int,
217â†’    private val maxDigits: Int,

229â†’            val ref = "secondFraction" // can't directly reference `secondFraction` due to resolution ambiguity
230â†’            // we ignore `grouping`, as it's not representable in the end users' code

232â†’                minDigits == 1 && maxDigits == 9 -> "$ref()"
233â†’                minDigits == 1 -> "$ref(maxLength = $maxDigits)"

238â†’        }

241â†’        other is FractionalSecondDirective && minDigits == other.minDigits && maxDigits == other.maxDigits

 92â†’internal fun LocalDateTime.until(other: LocalDateTime, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): Int {

 96â†’    } else if (endDate < date && other.time > time) {
 97â†’        endDate = endDate.plusDays(1) // won't throw: date - endDate >= 1

105â†’// org.threeten.bp.LocalDateTime#until
106â†’/** @throws ArithmeticException on arithmetic overflow. */
107â†’internal fun LocalDateTime.until(other: LocalDateTime, unit: DateTimeUnit.TimeBased): Long {
108â†’    val daysUntil = date.daysUntil(other.date)
109â†’    val timeUntil: Long = other.time.toNanosecondOfDay() - time.toNanosecondOfDay()

113â†’// org.threeten.bp.LocalDateTime#plusWithOverflow
114â†’/**
115â†’ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the result exceeds the boundaries
116â†’ * @throws ArithmeticException if arithmetic overflow occurs
117â†’ */
118â†’internal fun LocalDateTime.plusSeconds(seconds: Int): LocalDateTime
119â†’{
120â†’    if (seconds == 0) {
121â†’        return this
122â†’    }
123â†’    val currentNanoOfDay = time.toNanosecondOfDay() // at most a day
124â†’    val totalNanos: Long = seconds % SECONDS_PER_DAY * NANOS_PER_ONE.toLong() + // at most a day
125â†’        currentNanoOfDay
126â†’    val totalDays = seconds / SECONDS_PER_DAY + // max/24*60*60 < max * 0.000012
127â†’        totalNanos.floorDiv(NANOS_PER_DAY) // max 2 days
128â†’    val newNanoOfDay: Long = totalNanos.mod(NANOS_PER_DAY)
129â†’    val newTime: LocalTime = if (newNanoOfDay == currentNanoOfDay) time else LocalTime.ofNanoOfDay(newNanoOfDay)
130â†’    return LocalDateTime(date.plusDays(totalDays.toInt()), newTime)

 24â†’    init {

 43â†’

 50â†’        // org.threeten.bp.LocalTime#ofSecondOfDay(long, int)
 51â†’        internal fun ofSecondOfDay(secondOfDay: Int, nanoOfSecond: Int): LocalTime {
 52â†’            require(secondOfDay in 0 until SECONDS_PER_DAY)
 53â†’            require(nanoOfSecond in 0 until NANOS_PER_ONE)
 54â†’            val hours = (secondOfDay / SECONDS_PER_HOUR)
 55â†’            val secondWithoutHours = secondOfDay - hours * SECONDS_PER_HOUR
 56â†’            val minutes = (secondWithoutHours / SECONDS_PER_MINUTE)
 57â†’            val second = secondWithoutHours - minutes * SECONDS_PER_MINUTE
 58â†’            return LocalTime(hours, minutes, second, nanoOfSecond)

 60â†’
 61â†’        internal fun of(hour: Int, minute: Int, second: Int, nanosecond: Int): LocalTime {

 65â†’        // org.threeten.bp.LocalTime#ofNanoOfDay
 66â†’        internal fun ofNanoOfDay(nanoOfDay: Long): LocalTime {
 67â†’            require(nanoOfDay >= 0 && nanoOfDay < SECONDS_PER_DAY.toLong() * NANOS_PER_ONE)

 69â†’            val hours = (newNanoOfDay / NANOS_PER_HOUR).toInt()
 70â†’            newNanoOfDay -= hours * NANOS_PER_HOUR
 71â†’            val minutes = (newNanoOfDay / NANOS_PER_MINUTE).toInt()
 72â†’            newNanoOfDay -= minutes * NANOS_PER_MINUTE
 73â†’            val seconds = (newNanoOfDay / NANOS_PER_ONE).toInt()
 74â†’            newNanoOfDay -= seconds * NANOS_PER_ONE
 75â†’            return LocalTime(hours, minutes, seconds, newNanoOfDay.toInt())

 93â†’        if (h != 0) {
 94â†’            return h
 95â†’        }
 96â†’        val m = minute.compareTo(other.minute)
 97â†’        if (m != 0) {
 98â†’            return m
 99â†’        }
100â†’        val s = second.compareTo(other.second)
101â†’        if (s != 0) {
102â†’            return s

122â†’
123â†’    // org.threeten.bp.LocalTime#toNanoOfDay
124â†’    public actual fun toNanosecondOfDay(): Long {
125â†’        var total: Long = hour.toLong() * NANOS_PER_ONE * SECONDS_PER_HOUR
126â†’        total += minute.toLong() * NANOS_PER_ONE * SECONDS_PER_MINUTE
127â†’        total += second.toLong() * NANOS_PER_ONE

137â†’}

 22â†’private fun isValidYear(year: Int): Boolean =

 25â†’@Serializable(with = LocalDateIso8601Serializer::class)
 26â†’public actual class LocalDate actual constructor(public actual val year: Int, public actual val monthNumber: Int, public actual val dayOfMonth: Int) : Comparable<LocalDate> {

 31â†’        require(monthNumber in 1..12) { "Invalid date: month must be a number between 1 and 12, got $monthNumber" }
 32â†’        require(dayOfMonth in 1..31) { "Invalid date: day of month must be a number between 1 and 31, got $dayOfMonth" }

 38â†’            }
 39â†’        }
 40â†’    }

 51â†’        public actual fun fromEpochDays(epochDays: Int): LocalDate {
 52â†’            // LocalDate(-999999, 1, 1).toEpochDay(), LocalDate(999999, 12, 31).toEpochDay()
 53â†’            require(epochDays in MIN_EPOCH_DAY..MAX_EPOCH_DAY) {

 57â†’            // find the march-based year
 58â†’            zeroDay -= 60 // adjust to 0000-03-01 so leap day is at end of four year cycle
 59â†’
 60â†’            var adjust = 0
 61â†’            if (zeroDay < 0) { // adjust negative years to positive for calculation
 62â†’                val adjustCycles = (zeroDay + 1) / DAYS_PER_CYCLE - 1
 63â†’                adjust = adjustCycles * 400
 64â†’                zeroDay += -adjustCycles * DAYS_PER_CYCLE

 66â†’            var yearEst = ((400 * zeroDay.toLong() + 591) / DAYS_PER_CYCLE).toInt()

 68â†’            if (doyEst < 0) { // fix estimate
 69â†’                yearEst--
 70â†’                doyEst = zeroDay - (365 * yearEst + yearEst / 4 - yearEst / 100 + yearEst / 400)
 71â†’            }
 72â†’            yearEst += adjust // reset any negative year
 73â†’
 74â†’            val marchDoy0 = doyEst
 75â†’
 76â†’            // convert march-based values back to january-based
 77â†’            val marchMonth0 = (marchDoy0 * 5 + 2) / 153
 78â†’            val month = (marchMonth0 + 2) % 12 + 1
 79â†’            val dom = marchDoy0 - (marchMonth0 * 306 + 5) / 10 + 1

102â†’    // org.threeten.bp.LocalDate#toEpochDay
103â†’    public actual fun toEpochDays(): Int {
104â†’        val y = year
105â†’        val m = monthNumber
106â†’        var total = 0

108â†’        if (y >= 0) {
